fmt::Display::fmt(&self.name, fmt)\n    }\n}\n\n/// The constituent parts of a type level constant of kind ADT or array.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, HashStable)]\npub struct DestructuredConst<'tcx> {\n    pub variant: Option<VariantIdx>,\n    pub fields: &'tcx [ty::Const<'tcx>],\n}\n\n/// Generate TypeTree information for autodiff.\n/// This function creates TypeTree metadata that describes the memory layout\n/// of function parameters and return types for Enzyme autodiff.\npub fn fnc_typetrees<'tcx>(tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>, fn_ty: Ty<'tcx>) -> FncTree {\n    // Check if TypeTrees are disabled via NoTT flag\n    if tcx.sess.opts.unstable_opts.autodiff.contains(&rustc_session::config::AutoDiff::NoTT) {\n        return FncTree { args: vec![], ret: TypeTree::new() };\n    }\n\n    // Check if this is actually a function type\n    if !fn_ty.is_fn() {\n        return FncTree { args: vec![], ret: TypeTree::new() };\n    }\n\n    // Get the function signature\n    let fn_sig = fn_ty.fn_sig(tcx);\n    let sig = tcx.instantiate_bound_regions_with_erased(fn_sig);\n\n    // Create TypeTrees for each input parameter\n    let mut args = vec![];\n    for ty in sig.inputs().iter() {\n        let type_tree = typetree_from_ty(tcx, *ty);\n        args.push(type_tree);\n    }\n\n    // Create TypeTree for return type\n    let ret = typetree_from_ty(tcx, sig.output());\n\n    FncTree { args, ret }\n}\n\n/// Generate TypeTree for a specific type.\n/// This function analyzes a Rust type and creates appropriate TypeTree metadata.\npub fn typetree_from_ty<'tcx>(tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty: Ty<'tcx>) -> TypeTree {\n    let mut visited = Vec::new();\n    typetree_from_ty_inner(tcx, ty, 0, &mut visited)\n}\n\n/// Maximum recursion depth for TypeTree generation to prevent stack overflow\n/// from pathological deeply nested types. Combined with cycle detection.\nconst MAX_TYPETREE_DEPTH: usize = 6;\n\n/// Internal recursive function for TypeTree generation with cycle detection and depth limiting.\nfn typetree_from_ty_inner<'tcx>(\n    tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n    ty: Ty<'tcx>,\n    depth: usize,\n    visited: &mut Vec<Ty<'tcx>>,\n) -> TypeTree {\n    if depth >= MAX_TYPETREE_DEPTH {\n        trace!(\"typetree depth limit {} reached for type: {}\", MAX_TYPETREE_DEPTH, ty);\n        return TypeTree::new();\n    }\n\n    if visited.contains(&ty) {\n        return TypeTree::new();\n    }\n\n    visited.push(ty);\n    let result = typetree_from_ty_impl(tcx, ty, depth, visited);\n    visited.pop();\n    result\n}\n\n/// Implementation of TypeTree generation logic.\nfn typetree_from_ty_impl<'tcx>(\n    tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n    ty: Ty<'tcx>,\n    depth: usize,\n    visited: &mut Vec<Ty<'tcx>>,\n) -> TypeTree {\n    typetree_from_ty_impl_inner(tcx, ty, depth, visited, false)\n}\n\n/// Internal implementation with context about whether this is for a reference target.\nfn typetree_from_ty_impl_inner<'tcx>(\n    tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n    ty: Ty<'tcx>,\n    depth: usize,\n    visited: &mut Vec<Ty<'tcx>>,\n    is_reference_target: bool,\n) -> TypeTree {\n    if ty.is_scalar() {\n        let (kind, size) = if ty.is_integral() || ty.is_char() || ty.is_bool() {\n            (Kind::Integer, ty.primitive_size(tcx).bytes_usize())\n        } else if ty.is_floating_point() {\n            match ty {\n                x if x == tcx.types.f16 => (Kind::Half, 2),\n                x if x == tcx.types.f32 => (Kind::Float, 4),\n                x if x == tcx.types.f64 => (Kind::Double, 8),\n                x if x == tcx.types.f128 => (Kind::F128, 16),\n                _ => (Kind::Integer, 0),\n            }\n        } else {\n            (Kind::Integer, 0)\n        };\n\n        // Use offset 0 for scalars that are direct targets of references (like &f64)\n        // Use offset -1 for scalars used directly (like function return types)\n        let offset = if is_reference_target && !ty.is_array() { 0 } else { -1 };\n        return TypeTree(vec![Type { offset, size, kind, child: TypeTree::new() }]);\n    }\n\n    if ty.is_ref() || ty.is_raw_ptr() || ty.is_box() {\n        let inner_ty = if let Some(inner) = ty.builtin_deref(true) {\n            inner\n        } else {\n            return TypeTree::new();\n        };\n\n        let child = typetree_from_ty_impl_inner(tcx, inner_ty, depth + 1, visited, true);\n        return TypeTree(vec![Type {\n            offset: -1,\n            size: tcx.data_layout.pointer_size().bytes_usize(),\n            kind: Kind::Pointer,\n            child,\n        }]);\n    }\n\n    if ty.is_array() {\n        if let ty::Array(element_ty, len_const) = ty.kind() {\n            let len = len_const.try_to_target_usize(tcx).unwrap_or(0);\n            if len == 0 {\n                return TypeTree::new();\n            }\n            let element_tree =\n                typetree_from_ty_impl_inner(tcx, *element_ty, depth + 1, visited, false);\n            let mut types = Vec::new();\n            for elem_type in &element_tree.0 {\n                types.push(Type {\n                    offset: -1,\n                    size: elem_type.size,\n                    kind: elem_type.kind,\n                    child: elem_type.child.clone(),\n                });\n            }\n\n            return TypeTree(types);\n        }\n    }\n\n    if ty.is_slice() {\n        if let ty::Slice(element_ty) = ty.kind() {\n            let element_tree =\n                typetree_from_ty_impl_inner(tcx, *element_ty, depth + 1, visited, false);\n            return element_tree;\n        }\n    }\n\n    if let ty::Tuple(tuple_types) = ty.kind() {\n        if tuple_types.is_empty() {\n            return TypeTree::new();\n        }\n\n        let mut types = Vec::new();\n        let mut current_offset = 0;\n\n        for tuple_ty in tuple_types.iter() {\n            let element_tree =\n                typetree_from_ty_impl_inner(tcx, tuple_ty, depth + 1, visited, false);\n\n            let element_layout = tcx\n                .layout_of(ty::TypingEnv::fully_monomorphized().as_query_input(tuple_ty))\n                .ok()\n                .map(|layout| layout.size.bytes_usize())\n                .unwrap_or(0);\n\n            for elem_type in &element_tree.0 {\n                types.push(Type {\n                    offset: if elem_type.offset == -1 {\n                        current_offset as isize\n                    } else {\n                        current_offset as isize + elem_type.offset\n                    },\n                    size: elem_type.size,\n                    kind: elem_type.kind,\n                    child: elem_type.child.clone(),\n                });\n            }\n\n            current_offset += element_layout;\n        }\n\n        return TypeTree(types);\n    }\n\n    if let ty::Adt(adt_def, args) = ty.kind() {\n        if adt_def.is_struct() {\n            let struct_layout =\n                tcx.layout_of(ty::TypingEnv::fully_monomorphized().as_query_input(ty));\n            if let Ok(layout) = struct_layout {\n                let mut types = Vec::new();\n\n                for (field_idx, field_def) in adt_def.all_fields().enumerate() {\n                    let field_ty = field_def.ty(tcx, args);\n                    let field_tree =\n                        typetree_from_ty_impl_inner(tcx, field_ty, depth + 1, visited, false);\n\n                    let field_offset = layout.fields.offset(field_idx).bytes_usize();\n\n                    for elem_type in &field_tree.0 {\n                        types.push(Type {\n                            offset: if elem_type.offset == -1 {\n                                field_offset as isize\n                            } else {\n                                field_offset as isize + elem_type.offset\n                            },\n                            size: elem_type.size,\n                            kind: elem_type.kind,\n                            child: elem_type.child.clone(),\n                        });\n                    }\n                }\n\n                return TypeTree(types);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    TypeTree::new()\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":89,"oldLines":9,"newStart":89,"newLines":9,"lines":["     ExistentialPredicateStableCmpExt, match self.rigid()? {\n            RigidTy::Adt(def, args) if def.is_box() => {\n                Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *args.0.first()?.ty()?, mutability: Mutability::Not })\n            }\n TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnPtr(sig)) => Some(sig.clone()),\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Closure(_def, args)) => Some(with(|cx| cx.closure_sig(args))),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n       IntTy::Isize => MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes(),\n            IntTy::I8 => 1,\n            IntTy::I16 => 2,\n            IntTy::I32 => 4,\n            IntTy::I64 => 8,\n            IntTy::I128 => 16,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\npub enum UintTy {\n    Usize,\n    U8,\n    U16,\n    U32,\n    U64,\n    U128,\n}\n\nimpl UintTy {\n    pub fn num_bytes(self) -> usize {\n        match self {\n            UintTy::Usize => MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes(),\n            UintTy::U8 => 1,\n            UintTy::U16 => 2,\n {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub AdtDef;\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Hash, Serialize)]\npub enum AdtKind {\n    Enum,\n    Union,\n    Struct,\n}\n\nimpl AdtDef {\n    pub fn adt_def: *self })\n    }\n\n    pub fn variant(&self, idx: VariantIdx) -> Option<VariantDef> {\n        (idx.to_index() < self.num_variants()).then_some(VariantDef { idx, adt_def: *self })\n    }\n\n GenericArgKind {\n    /// Panic if this generic argument is not a type, otherwise\n    /// return the type.\n    #[track_caller]\n    pub fn expect_ty(&self) -> &Ty {\n        match self {\n            GenericArgKind::Type(ty) => ty,\n            _ => panic!(\"{self:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Panic if this generic argument is not a const, otherwise\n    /// return the const.\n    #[track_caller]\n    pub fn expect_const(&self) -> &TyConst {\n        match self {\n            GenericArgKind::Const(c) => c,\n            _ => panic!(\"{self:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Unadjusted,\n    RustCold,\n    RiscvInterruptM,\n    RiscvInterruptS,\n    RustInvalid,\n    Custom,\n}\n\n/// A binder represents a possibly generic type and its bound vars.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct Binder<T> {\n    pub value: T,\n    pub bound_vars: Vec<BoundVariableKind>,\n}\n\nimpl<T> Binder<T> {\n    /// Create a new binder with the given bound vars.\n    pub fn bind_with_vars(value: T, bound_vars: Vec<BoundVariableKind>) -> Self {\n        Binder { value, bound_vars }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new binder with no bounded variable.\n    pub fn dummy(value: T) -> Self {\n        Binder { value, bound_vars: vec![] }\n    }\n\n    pub fn skip_binder(self) -> T {\n        self.value\n    }\n\n    pub fn map_bound_ref<F, U>(&self, f: F) -> Binder<U>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(&T) -> U,\n    {\n        let Binder { value, bound_vars } = self;\n        let new_value = f(value);\n        Binder { value: new_value, bound_vars: bound_vars.clone() }\n    }\n\n    pub fn map_bound<F, U>(self, f: F) -> Binder<U>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(T) -> U,\n    {\n        let Binder { value, bound_vars } = self;\n        let new_value = f(value);\n        Binder { value: new_value, bound_vars }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct EarlyBinder<T> {\n    pub value: Vec<Option<u8>>;\n\n/// Size in bytes.\npub type Size = usize;\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct Prov(pub AllocId);\n\npub type Align = u64;\npub type Promoted = u32;\npub type InitMaskMaterialized = Vec<u64>;\n\n/// Stores the provenance information of pointers stored in memory.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct ProvenanceMap {\n    /// Provenance in this map applies from the given offset for an entire pointer-size worth of\n    /// bytes. Two entries in this map are always at least a pointer size apart.\n    pub ptrs: Vec<(Size, Prov)>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct Allocation {\n    pub bytes: Bytes,\n    pub provenance: ProvenanceMap,\n    pub align: Align,\n    pub mutability: Mutability,\n}\n\nimpl Allocation {\n    /// Get a vector of bytes for an Allocation that has been fully initialized\n    pub fn raw_bytes(&self) -> Result<Vec<u8>, Error> {\n        self.bytes\n            .iter()\n            .copied()\n            .collect::<Option<Vec<_>>>()\n            .ok_or_else(|| error!(\"Found uninitialized bytes: `{:?}`\", self.bytes))\n    }\n\n    /// Read a uint value from the specified range.\n    pub fn read_partial_uint(&self, range: Range<usize>) -> Result<u128, Error> {\n        if range.end - range.start > 16 {\n            return Err(error!(\"Allocation is bigger than largest integer\"));\n        }\n        if range.end > self.bytes.len() {\n            return Err(error!(\n                \"Range is out of bounds. Allocation length is `{}`, but requested range `{:?}`\",\n                self.bytes.len(),\n                range\n            ));\n        }\n        let raw = self.bytes[range]\n            .iter()\n            .copied()\n            .collect::<Option<Vec<_>>>()\n            .ok_or_else(|| error!(\"Found uninitialized bytes: `{:?}`\", self.bytes))?;\n        read_target_uint(&raw)\n    }\n\n    /// Read this allocation and try to convert it to an unassigned integer.\n    pub fn read_uint(&self) -> Result<u128, Error> {\n        if self.bytes.len() > 16 {\n            return Err(error!(\"Allocation is bigger than largest integer\"));\n        }\n        let raw = self.raw_bytes()?;\n        read_target_uint(&raw)\n    }\n\n    /// Read this allocation and try to convert it to a signed integer.\n    pub fn read_int(&self) -> Result<i128, Error> {\n        if self.bytes.len() > 16 {\n            return Err(error!(\"Allocation is bigger than largest integer\"));\n        }\n        let raw = self.raw_bytes()?;\n        read_target_int(&raw)\n    }\n\n    /// Read this allocation and try to convert it to a boolean.\n    pub fn read_bool(&self) -> Result<bool, Error> {\n        match self.read_int()? {\n            0 => Ok(false),\n            1 => Ok(true),\n            val => Err(error!(\"Unexpected value for bool: `{val}`\")),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Read this allocation as a pointer and return whether it represents a `null` pointer.\n    pub fn is_null(&self) -> Result<bool, Error> {\n        let len = self.bytes.len();\n        let ptr_len = MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes();\n        if len != ptr_len {\n            return Err(error!(\"Expected width of pointer (`{ptr_len}`), but found: `{len}`\"));\n        }\n        Ok(self.read_uint()? == 0 && self.provenance.ptrs.is_empty())\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub enum ConstantKind {\n    Ty(TyConst),\n PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct TraitRef {\n    pub def_id: TraitDef,\n    /// The generic arguments for this definition.\n    /// The first element must always be type, and it represents `Self`.\n    args: GenericArgs,\n}\n\nimpl TraitRef {\n    pub fn new(def_id: TraitDef, self_ty: Ty, gen_args: &GenericArgs) -> TraitRef {\n        let mut args = vec![GenericArgKind::Type(self_ty)];\n        args.extend_from_slice(&gen_args.0);\n        TraitRef { def_id, args: GenericArgs(args) }\n    }\n\n    pub fn try_new(def_id: TraitDef, args: GenericArgs) -> Result<TraitRef, ()> {\n        match &args.0[..] {\n            [GenericArgKind::Type(_), ..] => Ok(TraitRef { def_id, args }),\n            _ => Err(()),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn args(&self) -> &GenericArgs {\n        &self.args\n    }\n\n    pub fn self_ty(&self) -> Ty {\n        let GenericArgKind::Type(self_ty) = self.args.0[0] else {\n            panic!(\"Self must be a type, but found: {:?}\", self.args.0[0])\n        };\n        self_ty\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct Generics {\n    pub parent: Option<GenericDef>,\n    pub parent_count: usize,\n   Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *ty, mutability: *mutability })\n            }\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the function signature for function like types (Fn, FnPtr, and Closure)\n    pub fn fn_sig(&self) -> Option<PolyFnSig> {\n        match self {\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(def, args)) => Some(with(|cx| cx.fn_sig(*def, args))),\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnPtr(sig)) => Some(sig.clone()),\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Closure(_def, args)) => Some(with(|cx| cx.closure_sig(args))),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the discriminant type for this type.\n    pub fn discriminant_ty(&self) -> Option<Ty> {\n        self.rigid().map(|ty| with(|cx| cx.rigid_ty_discriminant_ty(ty)))\n    }\n\n    /// Deconstruct a function type if this is one.\n    pub fn fn_def(&self) -> Option<(FnDef, &GenericArgs)> {\n        if let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(def, args)) = self {\n            Some((*def, args))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\npub struct TypeAndMut {\n    pub ty: Ty,\n    pub mutability: Mutability,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub enum RigidTy {\n    Bool,\n    Char,\n    Int(IntTy),\n    Uint(UintTy),\n    Float(FloatTy),\n    Adt(AdtDef, GenericArgs),\n    Foreign(ForeignDef),\n    Str,\n    Array(Ty, TyConst),\n    Pat(Ty, Pattern),\n    Slice(Ty),\n    RawPtr(Ty, Mutability),\n    Ref(Region, Ty, Mutability),\n    FnDef(FnDef, GenericArgs),\n    FnPtr(PolyFnSig),\n    Closure(ClosureDef, GenericArgs),\n    Coroutine(CoroutineDef, GenericArgs),\n    CoroutineClosure(CoroutineClosureDef, GenericArgs),\n    Dynamic(Vec<Binder<ExistentialPredicate>>, Region),\n    Never,\n    Tuple(Vec<Ty>),\n    CoroutineWitness(CoroutineWitnessDef, GenericArgs),\n}\n\nimpl RigidTy {\n    /// Get the discriminant type for this type.\n    pub fn discriminant_ty(&self) -> Ty {\n        with(|cx| cx.rigid_ty_discriminant_ty(self))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<RigidTy> for TyKind {\n    fn from(value: RigidTy) -> Self {\n        TyKind::RigidTy(value)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\npub enum IntTy {\n    Isize,\n    I8,\n    I16,\n    I32,\n    I64,\n    I128,\n}\n\nimpl IntTy {\n    pub fn num_bytes(self) -> usize {\n        match self {\n            IntTy::Isize => MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes(),\n            IntTy::I8 => 1,\n            IntTy::I16 => 2,\n            IntTy::I32 => 4,\n            IntTy::I64 => 8,\n            IntTy::I128 => 16,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\npub enum UintTy {\n    Usize,\n    U8,\n    U16,\n    U32,\n    U64,\n    U128,\n}\n\nimpl UintTy {\n    pub fn num_bytes(self) -> usize {\n        match self {\n            UintTy::Usize => MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes(),\n            UintTy::U8 => 1,\n            UintTy::U16 => 2,\n            UintTy::U32 => 4,\n            UintTy::U64 => 8,\n            UintTy::U128 => 16,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\npub enum FloatTy {\n    F16,\n    F32,\n    F64,\n    F128,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\npub enum Movability {\n    Static,\n    Movable,\n}\n\ncrate_def! {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub ForeignModuleDef;\n}\n\nimpl ForeignModuleDef {\n    pub fn module(&self) -> ForeignModule {\n        with(|cx| cx.foreign_module(*self))\n    }\n}\n\npub struct ForeignModule {\n    pub def_id: /// Hold information about a ForeignItem in a crate.\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub ForeignDef;\n}\n\nimpl ForeignDef {\n    pub fn kind(&self) -> ForeignItemKind {\n        with(|cx| cx.foreign_item_kind(*self))\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Hash, Serialize)]\npub enum ForeignItemKind {\n    Fn(FnDef),\n    Static(StaticDef),\n    Type(Ty),\n}\n\ncrate_def_with_ty! {\n    /// Hold information about a function definition in a crate.\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub FnDef;\n}\n\nimpl FnDef {\n    // Get the function body if available.\n    pub fn body(&self) -> Option<Body> {\n        with(|ctx| ctx.has_body(self.0).then(|| ctx.mir_body(self.0)))\n    }\n\n    // Check if the function body is available.\n    pub fn has_body(&self) -> bool {\n        with(|ctx| ctx.has_body(self.0))\n    }\n\n    /// Get the information of the intrinsic if this function is a definition of one.\n    pub fn as_intrinsic(&self) -> Option<IntrinsicDef> {\n        with(|cx| cx.intrinsic(self.def_id()))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the function is an intrinsic.\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_intrinsic(&self) -> bool {\n        self.as_intrinsic().is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the function signature for this function definition.\n    pub fn fn_sig(&self) -> PolyFnSig {\n        let kind = self.ty().kind();\n        kind.fn_sig().unwrap()\n    }\n}\n\ncrate_def_with_ty! {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub IntrinsicDef;\n}\n\nimpl IntrinsicDef {\n    /// Returns the plain name of the intrinsic.\n    /// e.g., body and all backends\n    /// need to shim all calls to it.\n    pub fn must_be_overridden(&self) -> bool {\n        with(|cx| !cx.has_body(self.0))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<IntrinsicDef> for FnDef {\n {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub AdtDef;\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Hash, Serialize)]\npub enum AdtKind {\n    Enum,\n    Union,\n    Struct,\n}\n\nimpl AdtDef {\n    pub fn cx.adt_variants_len(*self))\n    }\n\n    /// Retrieve the variants in this ADT.\n    pub fn variants(&self) -> Vec<VariantDef> {\n        self.variants_iter().collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Iterate over the variants in this ADT.\n    pub fn variants_iter(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = VariantDef> {\n        (0..self.num_variants())\n            .map(|idx| VariantDef { idx: VariantIdx::to_val(idx), adt_def: *self })\n    }\n\n    pub fn variant(&self, idx: VariantIdx) -> Option<VariantDef> {\n        (idx.to_index() < self.num_variants()).then_some(VariantDef { idx, adt_def: *self })\n    }\n\n    pub fn repr(&self) -> ReprOptions {\n        with(|cx| cx.adt_repr(*self))\n    }\n\n    pub fn discriminant_for_variant(&self, idx: VariantIdx) -> Discr {\n        with(|cx| cx.adt_discr_for_variant(*self, idx))\n    }\n}\n\npub struct Discr {\n    pub val: u128,\n    pub ty: Ty,\n}\n\n/// Definition of a variant, which can be either a struct / union field or an enum variant.\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct VariantDef {\n    /// The variant index.\n    ///\n    /// ## Warning\n    /// Do not access this field directly!\n    pub idx: VariantIdx,\n    /// The data type where this variant comes from.\n    /// For now, we use this to retrieve information about the variant itself so we don't need to\n    /// cache more information.\n    ///\n    /// ## Warning\n    /// Do not access this field directly!\n    pub adt_def: AdtDef,\n}\n\nimpl VariantDef {\n    pub fn name(&self) -> Symbol {\n        with(|cx| cx.variant_name(*self))\n    }\n\n    /// Retrieve all the fields in this variant.\n    // We expect user to cache this and use it directly since today it is expensive to generate all\n    // fields name.\n    pub fn fields(&self) -> Vec<FieldDef> {\n        with(|cx| cx.variant_fields(*self))\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct FieldDef {\n    /// The field Ty {\n        with(|cx| cx.def_ty_with_args(self.def, args))\n    }\n\n    /// Retrieve the type of this field.\n    pub fn ty(&self) -> Ty {\n        with(|cx| cx.def_ty(self.def))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Display for AdtKind {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        f.write_str(match self {\n            AdtKind::Enum => \"enum\",\n            AdtKind::Union => \"union\",\n            AdtKind::Struct => \"struct\",\n        })\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AdtKind {\n    pub fn is_enum(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, AdtKind::Enum)\n    }\n\n    pub fn is_struct(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, AdtKind::Struct)\n    }\n\n    pub fn is_union(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, AdtKind::Union)\n    }\n}\n\ncrate_def! {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub ConstDef;\n}\n\ncrate_def! {\n    /// A trait impl definition.\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub ImplDef;\n}\n\nimpl_crate_def_items! {\n    ImplDef;\n}\n\nimpl ImplDef pub RegionDef;\n}\n\ncrate_def! {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub CoroutineWitnessDef;\n}\n\n/// A list of generic arguments.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct GenericArgs(pub Vec<GenericArgKind>);\n\nimpl std::ops::Index<ParamTy> for GenericArgs {\n    type Output = Ty;\n\n    fn index(&self, index: ParamTy) -> &Self::Output {\n        self.0[index.index as usize].expect_ty()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::ops::Index<ParamConst> for GenericArgs {\n    type Output = TyConst;\n\n    fn index(&self, index: ParamConst) -> &Self::Output {\n        self.0[index.index as usize].expect_const()\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub enum GenericArgKind {\n    Lifetime(Region),\n    Type(Ty),\n    Const(TyConst),\n}\n\nimpl GenericArgKind {\n    /// Panic if this generic argument is not a type, otherwise\n    /// return the type.\n    #[track_caller]\n    pub fn expect_ty(&self) -> &Ty {\n        match self {\n            GenericArgKind::Type(ty) => ty,\n            _ => panic!(\"{self:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Panic if this generic argument is not a const, otherwise\n    /// return the const.\n    #[track_caller]\n    pub fn expect_const(&self) -> &TyConst {\n        match self {\n            GenericArgKind::Const(c) => c,\n            _ => panic!(\"{self:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Return the generic argument type if applicable, otherwise return `None`.\n    pub fn ty(&self) -> Option<&Ty> {\n        match self {\n            GenericArgKind::Type(ty) => Some(ty),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub enum TermKind {\n    Type(Ty),\n    Const(TyConst),\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub PolyFnSig {\n    /// Compute a `FnAbi` suitable for indirect calls, i.e. to `fn` pointers.\n    ///\n    /// NB: this doesn't handle virtual calls - those should use `Instance::fn_abi`\n    /// instead, where the instance is an `InstanceKind::Virtual`.\n    pub fn fn_ptr_abi(self) -> Result<FnAbi, Error> {\n        with(|cx| cx.fn_ptr_abi(self))\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, Ty {\n        self.inputs_and_output[self.inputs_and_output.len() - 1]\n    }\n\n    pub fn inputs(&self) -> &[Ty] {\n        &self.inputs_and_output[..self.inputs_and_output.len() - 1]\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Serialize)]\npub enum Abi {\n    Rust,\n    C { unwind: bool },\n    Cdecl { unwind: bool },\n    Stdcall { unwind: bool },\n    Fastcall { unwind: bool },\n    Vectorcall { unwind: bool },\n    Thiscall { unwind: bool },\n    Aapcs { unwind: bool },\n    Win64 { unwind: bool },\n    SysV64 { unwind: bool },\n    PtxKernel,\n    Msp430Interrupt,\n    X86Interrupt,\n    GpuKernel,\n    EfiApi,\n    AvrInterrupt,\n    AvrNonBlockingInterrupt,\n    CCmseNonSecureCall,\n    CCmseNonSecureEntry,\n    System { unwind: bool },\n    RustCall,\n    Unadjusted,\n    RustCold,\n    RiscvInterruptM,\n    RiscvInterruptS,\n    RustInvalid,\n    Custom,\n}\n\n/// A binder represents a possibly generic type and its bound vars.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct Binder<T> {\n    pub value: T,\n    pub bound_vars: Vec<BoundVariableKind>,\n}\n\nimpl<T> Binder<T> {\n    /// Create a new binder with the given bound vars.\n    pub fn bind_with_vars(value: T, bound_vars: Vec<BoundVariableKind>) -> Self {\n        Binder { value, bound_vars }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new binder with no bounded variable.\n    pub fn dummy(value: T) -> Self {\n        Binder { value, bound_vars: vec![] }\n    }\n\n    pub fn skip_binder(self) -> T {\n        self.value\n    }\n\n    pub fn map_bound_ref<F, U>(&self, f: F) -> Binder<U>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(&T) -> U,\n    {\n        let Binder { value, bound_vars } = self;\n        let new_value = f(value);\n        Binder { value: new_value, bound_vars: bound_vars.clone() }\n    }\n\n    pub fn map_bound<F, U>(self, f: F) -> Binder<U>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(T) -> U,\n    {\n        let Binder { value, bound_vars } = self;\n        let new_value = f(value);\n        Binder { value: new_value, bound_vars }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct EarlyBinder<T> {\n    pub value: T,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub enum BoundVariableKind {\n    Ty(BoundTyKind),\n    Region(BoundRegionKind),\n    Const,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, BrNamed(BrNamedDef, String),\n    BrEnv,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub enum ExistentialPredicate {\n    Trait(ExistentialTraitRef),\n    Projection(ExistentialProjection),\n    AutoTrait(TraitDef),\n}\n\n/// An existential reference to a trait where `Self` is not included.\n///\n/// The `generic_args` will include any other known argument.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct ExistentialTraitRef {\n    pub def_id: TraitDef,\n    pub generic_args: GenericArgs,\n}\n\nimpl Binder<ExistentialTraitRef> {\n    pub fn with_self_ty(&self, self_ty: Ty) -> Binder<TraitRef> {\n        self.map_bound_ref(|trait_ref| trait_ref.with_self_ty(self_ty))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ExistentialTraitRef {\n    pub fn ExistentialProjection {\n    pub def_id: TraitDef,\n    pub generic_args: GenericArgs,\n    pub term: TermKind,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct ParamTy {\n    pub index: u32,\n    pub name: String,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct BoundTy {\n    pub var: usize,\n    pub kind: BoundTyKind,\n}\n\npub type Bytes = Vec<Option<u8>>;\n\n/// Size in bytes.\npub type Size = usize;\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct Prov(pub AllocId);\n\npub type Align = u64;\npub type Promoted = u32;\npub type InitMaskMaterialized = Vec<u64>;\n\n/// Stores the provenance information of pointers stored in memory.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct ProvenanceMap {\n    /// Provenance in this map applies from the given offset for an entire pointer-size worth of\n    /// bytes. Two entries in this map are always at least a pointer size apart.\n    pub ptrs: Vec<(Size, Prov)>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct Allocation {\n    pub bytes: Bytes,\n    pub provenance: ProvenanceMap,\n    pub align: Align,\n    pub mutability: Mutability,\n}\n\nimpl Allocation {\n    /// Get a vector of bytes for an Allocation that has been fully initialized\n    pub fn raw_bytes(&self) -> Result<Vec<u8>, Error> {\n        self.bytes\n            .iter()\n            .copied()\n            .collect::<Option<Vec<_>>>()\n            .ok_or_else(|| error!(\"Found uninitialized bytes: `{:?}`\", self.bytes))\n    }\n\n    /// Read a uint value from the specified range.\n    pub fn read_partial_uint(&self, range: Range<usize>) -> Result<u128, Error> {\n        if range.end - range.start > 16 {\n            return Err(error!(\"Allocation is bigger than largest integer\"));\n        }\n        if range.end > self.bytes.len() {\n            return Err(error!(\n                \"Range is out of bounds. Allocation length is `{}`, but requested range `{:?}`\",\n                self.bytes.len(),\n                range\n            ));\n        }\n        let raw = self.bytes[range]\n            .iter()\n            .copied()\n            .collect::<Option<Vec<_>>>()\n            .ok_or_else(|| error!(\"Found uninitialized bytes: `{:?}`\", self.bytes))?;\n        read_target_uint(&raw)\n    }\n\n    /// Read this allocation and try to convert it to an unassigned integer.\n    pub fn read_uint(&self) -> Result<u128, Error> {\n        if self.bytes.len() > 16 {\n            return Err(error!(\"Allocation is bigger than largest integer\"));\n        }\n        let raw = self.raw_bytes()?;\n        read_target_uint(&raw)\n    }\n\n    /// Read this allocation and try to convert it to a signed integer.\n    pub fn read_int(&self) -> Result<i128, Error> {\n        if self.bytes.len() > 16 {\n            return Err(error!(\"Allocation is bigger than largest integer\"));\n        }\n        let raw = self.raw_bytes()?;\n        read_target_int(&raw)\n    }\n\n    /// Read this allocation and try to convert it to a boolean.\n    pub fn read_bool(&self) -> Result<bool, Error> {\n        match self.read_int()? {\n            0 => Ok(false),\n            1 => Ok(true),\n            val => Err(error!(\"Unexpected value for bool: `{val}`\")),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Read this allocation as a pointer and return whether it represents a `null` pointer.\n    pub fn is_null(&self) -> Result<bool, Error> {\n        let len = self.bytes.len();\n        let ptr_len = MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes();\n        if len != ptr_len {\n            return Err(error!(\"Expected width of pointer (`{ptr_len}`), but found: `{len}`\"));\n        }\n        Ok(self.read_uint()? == 0 && self.provenance.ptrs.is_empty())\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub enum ConstantKind {\n    Ty(TyConst),\n    Allocated(Allocation),\n    Unevaluated(UnevaluatedConst),\n    Param(ParamConst),\n    /// Store ZST constants.\n    /// We have to special handle these constants since its type might be generic.\n    ZeroSized,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct ParamConst {\n    pub index: u32,\n    pub name: String,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct UnevaluatedConst {\n    pub def: ConstDef,\n    pub args: GenericArgs,\n    pub promoted: Option<Promoted>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize)]\npub enum TraitSpecializationKind {\n    None,\n    Marker,\n    AlwaysApplicable,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct TraitDecl {\n    pub def_id: TraitDef,\n    pub safety: Safety,\n    pub paren_sugar: bool,\n    pub has_auto_impl: bool,\n    pub is_marker: bool,\n    pub is_coinductive: bool,\n    pub skip_array_during_method_dispatch: bool,\n    pub skip_boxed_slice_during_method_dispatch: bool,\n    pub specialization_kind: TraitSpecializationKind,\n    pub must_implement_one_of: Option<Vec<Ident>>,\n    pub implement_via_object: bool,\n    pub deny_explicit_impl: bool,\n}\n\nimpl TraitDecl {\n    pub fn generics_of(&self) -> Generics {\n        with(|cx| cx.generics_of(self.def_id.0))\n    }\n\n    pub fn predicates_of(&self) -> args.extend_from_slice(&gen_args.0);\n        TraitRef { def_id, args: GenericArgs(args) }\n    }\n\n    pub fn try_new(def_id: TraitDef, args: GenericArgs) -> Result<TraitRef, ()> {\n        match &self.args\n    }\n\n    pub fn self_ty(&self) -> Ty {\n        let GenericArgKind::Type(self_ty) = self.args.0[0] else {\n            panic!(\"Self must be a type, but found: {:?}\", T = V>,\n{\n    type T = crate::ty::EarlyBinder<V>;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::EarlyBinder;\n\n        EarlyBinder { value: self.as_ref().skip_binder().stable(tables, cx) }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::FnSig<'tcx> {\n    type T =       FnSig {\n            inputs_and_output: self\n                .inputs_and_output\n                .iter()\n                .map(|ty| ty.stable(tables, cx))\n                .collect(),\n            c_variadic: self.c_variadic,\n            safety: self.safety.stable(tables, cx),\n            abi: self.abi.stable(tables, cx),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::BoundTyKind use crate::ty::BoundTyKind;\n\n        match self {\n            ty::BoundTyKind::Anon => BoundTyKind::Anon,\n            ty::BoundTyKind::Param(def_id) => {\n crate::ty::BoundRegionKind;\n\n        match self {\n            ty::BoundRegionKind::Anon => BoundRegionKind::BrAnon,\n            ty::BoundRegionKind::Named(def_id) => BoundRegionKind::BrNamed(\n                tables.br_named_def(*def_id),\n                cx.tcx.item_name(*def_id).to_string(),\n            ),\n            ty::BoundRegionKind::ClosureEnv => BoundRegionKind::BrEnv,\n            ty::BoundRegionKind::NamedAnon(_) => bug!(\"only used for pretty printing\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::BoundVariableKind {\n    type T = crate::ty::BoundVariableKind;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::BoundVariableKind;\n\n        match self {\n            ty::BoundVariableKind::Ty(bound_ty_kind) => {\n                BoundVariableKind::Ty(bound_ty_kind.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            ty::BoundVariableKind::Region(bound_region_kind) => {\n                BoundVariableKind::Region(bound_region_kind.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            ty::BoundVariableKind::Const => BoundVariableKind::Const,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::IntTy {\n    type T = IntTy;\n\n    fn stable(&self, _: &mut Tables<'_, BridgeTys>, _:         ty::IntTy::I32 => IntTy::I32,\n            ty::IntTy::I64 => IntTy::I64,\n            ty::IntTy::I128 => IntTy::I128,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::UintTy {\n    type T = UintTy;\n\n    fn stable(&self, _: &mut Tables<'_, BridgeTys>, _: &CompilerCtxt<'_, BridgeTys>) -> Self::T {\n        match self {\n            ty::UintTy::Usize => UintTy::Usize,\n            ty::UintTy::U8 => UintTy::U8,\n            ty::UintTy::U16 => UintTy::U16,\n            ty::UintTy::U32 => UintTy::U32,\n            ty::UintTy::U64 => UintTy::U64,\n            ty::UintTy::U128 => ty::FloatTy::F128 => FloatTy::F128,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for Ty<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::Ty;\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        tables.intern_ty(cx.lift(*self).unwrap())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::TyKind<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::TyKind;\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        match self {\n            ty::Bool => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Bool),\n            ty::Char => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Char),\n            ty::Int(int_ty) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Int(int_ty.stable(tables, cx))),\n            ty::Uint(uint_ty) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Uint(uint_ty.stable(tables, cx))),\n            ty::Float(float_ty) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Float(float_ty.stable(tables, cx))),\n            ty::Adt(adt_def, generic_args) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(\n                tables.adt_def(adt_def.did()),\n                generic_args.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::Foreign(def_id) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Foreign(tables.foreign_def(*def_id))),\n            ty::Str => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Str),\n            ty::Array(ty, constant) => {\n                TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Array(ty.stable(tables, cx), constant.stable(tables, cx)))\n            }\n            ty::Pat(ty, pat) => {\n                TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Pat(ty.stable(tables, cx), pat.stable(tables, cx)))\n            }\n            ty::Slice(ty) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Slice(ty.stable(tables, cx))),\n            ty::RawPtr(ty, mutbl) => {\n                TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::RawPtr(ty.stable(tables, cx), mutbl.stable(tables, cx)))\n            }\n            ty::Ref(region, ty, mutbl) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Ref(\n                region.stable(tables, cx),\n                ty.stable(tables, cx),\n                mutbl.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::FnDef(def_id, generic_args) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(\n                tables.fn_def(*def_id),\n                generic_args.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::FnPtr(sig_tys, hdr) => {\n                TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnPtr(sig_tys.with(*hdr).stable(tables, cx)))\n            }\n            // FIXME(unsafe_binders):\n            ty::UnsafeBinder(_) => todo!(),\n            ty::Dynamic(existential_predicates, region) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Dynamic(\n                existential_predicates\n                    .iter()\n                    .map(|existential_predicate| existential_predicate.stable(tables, cx))\n                    .collect(),\n                region.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::Closure(def_id, generic_args) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Closure(\n                tables.closure_def(*def_id),\n                generic_args.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::CoroutineClosure(..) => todo!(\"FIXME(async_closures): Lower these to SMIR\"),\n            ty::Coroutine(def_id, generic_args) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Coroutine(\n                tables.coroutine_def(*def_id),\n                generic_args.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::Never => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Never),\n            ty::Tuple(fields) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Tuple(\n                fields.iter().map(|ty| ty.stable(tables, cx)).collect(),\n            )),\n            ty::Alias(alias_kind, alias_ty) => {\n                TyKind::Alias(alias_kind.stable(tables, cx), alias_ty.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            ty::Param(param_ty) => TyKind::Param(param_ty.stable(tables, cx)),\n            ty::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Canonical, _) => {\n                unreachable!()\n            }\n            ty::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Bound(debruijn_idx), bound_ty) => {\n                TyKind::Bound(debruijn_idx.as_usize(), bound_ty.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            ty::CoroutineWitness(def_id, args) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::CoroutineWitness(\n                tables.coroutine_witness_def(*def_id),\n                args.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::Placeholder(..) | ty::Infer(_) | ty::Error(_) => {\n                unreachable!();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::Pattern<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::Pattern;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        match **self {\n            ty::PatternKind::Range { start, end } => crate::ty::Pattern::Range {\n                // FIXME(SMIR): update data structures to not have an Option here anymore\n                start: Some(start.stable(tables, cx)),\n                end: Some(end.stable(tables, cx)),\n                include_end: true,\n            },\n            ty::PatternKind::NotNull => todo!(),\n            ty::PatternKind::Or(_) => todo!(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::Const<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::TyConst;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        let ct = cx.lift(*self).unwrap();\n        let kind = match ct.kind() {\n            ty::ConstKind::Value(cv) => {\n                let const_val = cx.valtree_to_const_val(cv);\n                if matches!(const_val, mir::ConstValue::ZeroSized) {\n                    crate::ty::TyConstKind::ZSTValue(cv.ty.stable(tables, cx))\n                } else {\n                    crate::ty::TyConstKind::Value(\n                        cv.ty.stable(tables, cx),\n                        alloc::new_allocation(cv.ty, const_val, tables, cx),\n                    )\n                }\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(param) => crate::ty::TyConstKind::Param(param.stable(tables, cx)),\n            ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => crate::ty::TyConstKind::Unevaluated(\n                tables.const_def(uv.def),\n                uv.args.stable(tables, cx),\n            ),\n            ty::ConstKind::Error(_) => unreachable!(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(_) => unreachable!(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Bound(_, _) => unimplemented!(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(_) => unimplemented!(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => unimplemented!(),\n        };\n        let id = tables.intern_ty_const(ct);\n        crate::ty::TyConst::new(kind, id)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::ParamConst {\n    type T = crate::ty::ParamConst;\n    fn stable(&self, _: &mut Tables<'_, BridgeTys>, _: &CompilerCtxt<'_, BridgeTys>) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::ParamConst;\n        ParamConst { index: self.index, name: self.name.to_string() }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for let param_def_id_to_index =\n            params.iter().map(|param| (param.def_id, param.index)).collect();\n\n        Generics {\n            parent: self.parent.map(|did| tables.generic_def(did)),\n            parent_count: self.parent_count,\n            params,\n            param_def_id_to_index,\n            has_self: self.has_self,\n            has_late_bound_regions: self\n                .has_late_bound_regions\n                .as_ref()\n                .map(|late_bound_regions| late_bound_regions.stable(tables, cx)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for Imagine you have a function `F: FnOnce(&[T]) -> R`, plus an iterator `iter`\n/// that produces `T` items. You could combine them with\n/// `f(&iter.collect::<Vec<_>>())`, but this requires allocating memory for the\n/// `Vec`.\n///\n/// This trait allows for faster implementations, intended for cases where the\n/// number of items produced by the iterator is small. There is a blanket impl\n/// for `T` items, but there is also a fallible impl for `Result<T, E>` items.\npub trait CollectAndApply<T, R>: Sized {\n    type Output;\n\n    /// Produce a result of type `Self::Output` from `iter`. The result will\n    /// typically be produced by applying `f` on the elements produced by\n    /// `iter`, though this may not happen in some impls, e.g. if an error\n    /// occurred during iteration.\n    fn collect_and_apply<I, F>(iter: I, f: F) -> Self::Output\n    where\n        I: Iterator<Item = Self>,\n        F: FnOnce(&[T]) -> R;\n}\n\n/// The blanket impl that always collects all elements and applies `f`.\nimpl<T, R> CollectAndApply<T, R> for T {\n    type Output = R;\n\n    /// Equivalent to `f(&iter.collect::<Vec<_>>())`.\n    fn collect_and_apply<I, F>(mut iter: I, f: F) -> R\n    where\n        I: Iterator<Item = T>,\n        F: FnOnce(&[T]) -> R,\n    {\n        // This code is hot enough that it's worth specializing for the most\n        // common length lists, to avoid the overhead of `Vec` creation.\n\n        let Some(t0) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[]);\n        };\n\n        let Some(t1) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[t0]);\n        };\n\n        let Some(t2) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[t0, t1]);\n        };\n\n        let Some(t3) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[t0, t1, t2]);\n        };\n\n        let Some(t4) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3]);\n        };\n\n        let Some(t5) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4]);\n        };\n\n        let Some(t6) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4, t5]);\n        };\n\n        let Some(t7) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4, t5, t6]);\n        };\n\n        let Some(t8) = iter.next() else {\n            return f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4, t5, t6, t7]);\n        };\n\n        f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4, t5, t6, t7, t8].into_iter().chain(iter).collect::<Vec<_>>())\n    }\n}\n\n/// A fallible impl that will fail, without calling `f`, if there are any\n/// errors during collection.\nimpl<T, R, E> CollectAndApply<T, R> for Result<T, E> {\n    type Output = Result<R, E>;\n\n    /// Equivalent to `Ok(f(&iter.collect::<Result<Vec<_>>>()?))`.\n    fn collect_and_apply<I, F>(mut iter: I, f: F) -> Result<R, E>\n    where\n        I: Iterator<Item = Result<T, E>>,\n        F: FnOnce(&[T]) -> R,\n    {\n        // This code is hot enough that it's worth specializing for the most\n        // common length lists, to avoid the overhead of `Vec` creation.\n\n        let Some(t0) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[]));\n        };\n        let t0 = t0?;\n\n        let Some(t1) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[t0]));\n        };\n        let t1 = t1?;\n\n        let Some(t2) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[t0, t1]));\n        };\n        let t2 = t2?;\n\n        let Some(t3) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[t0, t1, t2]));\n        };\n        let t3 = t3?;\n\n        let Some(t4) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3]));\n        };\n        let t4 = t4?;\n\n        let Some(t5) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4]));\n        };\n        let t5 = t5?;\n\n        let Some(t6) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4, t5]));\n        };\n        let t6 = t6?;\n\n        let Some(t7) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4, t5, t6]));\n        };\n        let t7 = t7?;\n\n        let Some(t8) = iter.next() else {\n            return Ok(f(&[t0, t1, t2, t3, t4, t5, t6, t7]));\n        };\n        let t8 = t8?;\n\n        Ok(f(&[Ok(t0), Ok(t1), Ok(t2), Ok(t3), Ok(t4), Ok(t5), Ok(t6), Ok(t7), Ok(t8)]\n            .into_iter()\n            .chain(iter)\n            .collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>()?))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<I: Interner> search_graph::Cx for I {\n    type Input = CanonicalInput<I>;\n    type Result = QueryResult<I>;\n    type AmbiguityInfo = Certainty;\n\n    type DepNodeIndex = I::DepNodeIndex;\n    type Tracked<T: Debug + Clone> = I::Tracked<T>;\n    fn mk_tracked<T: Debug + Clone>(\n        self,\n        data: T,\n        dep_node_index: I::DepNodeIndex,\n    ) -> I::Tracked<T> {\n        I::mk_tracked(self, data, dep_node_index)\n    }\n    fn get_tracked<T: Debug + Clone>(self, tracked: &I::Tracked<T>) -> T {\n        I::get_tracked(self, tracked)\n    }\n    fn with_cached_task<T>(self, task: impl FnOnce() -> T) -> (T, I::DepNodeIndex) {\n        I::with_cached_task(self, task)\n    }\n    fn with_global_cache<R>(self, f: impl FnOnce(&mut search_graph::GlobalCache<Self>) -> R) -> R {\n        I::with_global_cache(self, f)\n    }\n    fn assert_evaluation_is_concurrent(&self) {\n        self.assert_evaluation_is_concurrent()\n This isn't\n   736→        // *necessary* to prevent cycles, because they would eventually be sub-unified\n   737→        // anyhow during generalization, but it helps with diagnostics (we can detect\n   738→        // earlier that they are sub-unified).\n   739→        //\n   740→        // Note that we can just skip the binders here because\n   741→        // type variables can't (at present, at\n   742→        // least) capture any of the things bound by this binder.\n   743→        //\n   744→        // Note that this sub here is not just for diagnostics - it has semantic\n   745→        // effects as well.\n   746→        let r_a = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().a);\n   747→        let r_b = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().b);\n   748→        match (r_a.kind(), r_b.kind()) {\n   749→            (&ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), &ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n   750→                self.sub_unify_ty_vids_raw(a_vid, b_vid);\n   751→                return Err((a_vid, b_vid));\n   752→            }\n   753→            _ => {}\n   754→        }\n   755→\n   756→        self.enter_forall(predicate, |ty::SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected, a, b }| {\n   757→            if a_is_expected {\n   758→                Ok(self.at(cause, param_env).sub(DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes, a, b))\n   759→            } else {\n   760→                Ok(self.at(cause, param_env).sup(DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes, b, a))\n   761→            }\n   762→        })\n   763→    }\n   764→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. This is partly a micro-optimization, but it's\n        // also an opportunity to \"sub-unify\" the variables. This isn't\n        // *necessary* to prevent cycles, because they would eventually be sub-unified\n        // anyhow during generalization, but it helps with diagnostics (we can detect\n        // earlier that they are sub-unified).\n        //\n        // Note that we can just skip the binders here because\n        // type variables can't (at present, at\n        // least) capture any of the things bound by this binder.\n        //\n        // Note that this sub here is not just for diagnostics - it has semantic\n        // effects as well.\n        let r_a = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().a);\n        let r_b = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().b);\n        match (r_a.kind(), r_b.kind()) {\n            (&ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), &ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                self.sub_unify_ty_vids_raw(a_vid, b_vid);\n                return Err((a_vid, b_vid));\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n\n        self.enter_forall(predicate, |ty::SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected, a, b }| {\n            if a_is_expected {\n                Ok(self.at(cause, param_env).sub(DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes, a, b))\n            } else {\n                Ok(self.at(cause, param_env).sup(DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes, b, a))\n            }\n        })\n    }\n","numLines":50,"startLine":715,"totalLines":1697}}}

unify_key;\n\n/// `InferOk<'tcx, ()>` is used a lot. It may seem like a useless wrapper\n/// around `PredicateObligations<'tcx>`, but it has one important property:\n/// because `InferOk` is marked with `#[must_use]`, if you have a method\n/// `InferCtxt::f` that returns `InferResult<'tcx, ()>` and you call it with\n/// `infcx.f()?;` you'll get a warning about the obligations being discarded\n/// without use, which is probably unintentional and has been a source of bugs\n/// in the past.\n#[must_use]\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct InferOk<'tcx, T> {\n    pub value: T,\n    pub obligations: PredicateObligations<'tcx>,\n}\npub type InferResult<'tcx, T> = Result<InferOk<'tcx, T>, TypeError<'tcx>>;\n\npub(crate) type FixupResult<T> = Result<T, FixupError>; // \"fixup result\"\n\npub(crate) type UnificationTable<'a, 'tcx, T> = ut::UnificationTable<\n    ut::InPlace<T, &'a mut ut::UnificationStorage<T>, &'a mut InferCtxtUndoLogs<'tcx>>,\n>;\n\n/// This type contains all the things within `InferCtxt` that sit within a\n/// `RefCell` and are involved with taking/rolling back snapshots. Snapshot\n/// operations are hot enough that we want only one call to `borrow_mut` per\n/// call to `start_snapshot` and `rollback_to`.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct InferCtxtInner<'tcx> {\n    undo_log: InferCtxtUndoLogs<'tcx>,\n\n    /// Cache for projections.\n    ///\n    /// This cache is snapshotted along with the infcx.\n    projection_cache: traits::ProjectionCacheStorage<'tcx>,\n\n    /// We instantiate `UnificationTable` with `bounds<Ty>` because the types\n    /// that might instantiate a general type variable have an order,\n    /// represented by its upper and lower bounds.\n    type_variable_storage: type_variable::TypeVariableStorage<'tcx>,\n\n    /// Map from const parameter variable to the kind of const it represents.\n    const_unification_storage: ut::UnificationTableStorage<ConstVidKey<'tcx>>,\n\n    /// Map from integral variable to the kind of integer it represents.\n    int_unification_storage: ut::UnificationTableStorage<ty::IntVid>,\n\n    /// Map from floating variable to the kind of float it represents.\n    float_unification_storage: ut::UnificationTableStorage<ty::FloatVid>,\n\n    /// Tracks the set of region variables and the constraints between them.\n    ///\n    /// This is initially `Some(_)` but when\n    /// `resolve_regions_and_report_errors` is invoked, this gets set to `None`\n    /// -- further attempts to perform unification, etc., may fail if new\n    /// region constraints would've been added.\n    region_constraint_storage: Option<RegionConstraintStorage<'tcx>>,\n\n    /// A set of constraints that regionck must validate.\n    ///\n    /// Each constraint has the form `T:'a`, meaning \"some type `T` must\n    /// outlive the lifetime 'a\". These constraints derive from\n    /// instantiated type parameters. So if you had a struct defined\n    /// like the following:\n    /// ```ignore (illustrative)\n    /// struct Foo<T: 'static> { ... }\n    /// ```\n    /// In some expression `let x = Foo { ... }`, it will\n    /// instantiate the type parameter `T` with a fresh type `$0`. At\n    /// the same time, it will record a region obligation of\n    /// `$0: 'static`. This will get checked later by regionck. (We\n    /// can't generally check these things right away because we have\n    /// to wait until types are resolved.)\n    region_obligations: Vec<TypeOutlivesConstraint<'tcx>>,\n\n    /// The outlives bounds that we assume must hold about placeholders that\n    /// come from instantiating the binder of coroutine-witnesses. These bounds\n    /// are deduced from the well-formedness of the witness's types, and are\n    /// necessary because of the way we anonymize the regions in a coroutine,\n    /// which may cause types to no longer be considered well-formed.\n    region_assumptions: Vec<ty::ArgOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>>,\n\n    /// `-Znext-solver`: Successfully proven goals during HIR typeck which\n    /// reference inference variables and get reproven in case MIR type check\n    /// fails to prove something.\n    ///\n    /// See the documentation of `InferCtxt::in_hir_typeck` for more details.\n    hir_typeck_potentially_region_dependent_goals: Vec<PredicateObligation<'tcx>>,\n\n    /// Caches for opaque type inference.\n    opaque_type_storage: OpaqueTypeStorage<'tcx>,\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> InferCtxtInner<'tcx> {\n    fn new() -> InferCtxtInner<'tcx> {\n        InferCtxtInner {\n            undo_log: InferCtxtUndoLogs::default(),\n\n            projection_cache: Default::default(),\n            type_variable_storage: Default::default(),\n            const_unification_storage: Default::default(),\n            int_unification_storage: Default::default(),\n            float_unification_storage: Default::default(),\n            region_constraint_storage: Some(Default::default()),\n            region_obligations: Default::default(),\n            region_assumptions: Default::default(),\n            hir_typeck_potentially_region_dependent_goals: Default::default(),\n            opaque_type_storage: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn region_obligations(&self) -> &[TypeOutlivesConstraint<'tcx>] {\n        &self.region_obligations\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn region_assumptions(&self) -> &[ty::ArgOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>] {\n        &self.region_assumptions\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn projection_cache(&mut self) -> traits::ProjectionCache<'_, 'tcx> {\n        self.projection_cache.with_log(&mut self.undo_log)\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    fn try_type_variables_probe_ref(\n        &self,\n        vid: ty::TyVid,\n    ) -> Option<&type_variable::TypeVariableValue<'tcx>> {\n        // Uses a read-only view of the unification table, this way we don't\n        // need an undo log.\n        self.type_variable_storage.eq_relations_ref().try_probe_value(vid)\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    fn type_variables(&mut self) opaque_types::OpaqueTypeTable<'_, 'tcx> {\n        self.opaque_type_storage.with_log(&mut self.undo_log)\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    fn int_unification_table(&mut self) -> UnificationTable<'_, 'tcx,
