If it returns `true` the variable is\n    /// unchanged, `false` indicates that the status is unknown.\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_ty_infer_var_definitely_unchanged(&self) -> impl Fn(TyOrConstInferVar) -> bool {\n        // This hoists the borrow/release out of the loop body.\n        let inner = self.inner.try_borrow();\n\n        move |infer_var: TyOrConstInferVar| match (infer_var, &inner) {\n            (TyOrConstInferVar::Ty(ty_var), Ok(inner)) => {\n                use self::type_variable::TypeVariableValue;\n\n                matches!(\n                    inner.try_type_variables_probe_ref(ty_var),\n                    Some(TypeVariableValue::Unknown { .. })\n                )\n            }\n            _ => false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// `ty_or_const_infer_var_changed` is equivalent to one of these two:\n    ///   * `shallow_resolve(ty) != ty` (where `ty.kind = ty::Infer(_)`)\n    ///   * `shallow_resolve(ct) != ct` (where `ct.kind = ty::ConstKind::Infer(_)`)\n    ///\n    /// However, `ty_or_const_infer_var_changed` is more efficient. It's always\n    /// inlined, despite being large, because it has only two call sites that\n    /// are extremely hot (both in `traits::fulfill`'s checking of `stalled_on`\n    /// inference variables), and it handles both `Ty` and `ty::Const` without\n    /// having to resort to storing full `GenericArg`s in `stalled_on`.\n    #[inline(always)]\n    pub fn ty_or_const_infer_var_changed(&self, infer_var: TyOrConstInferVar) -> bool {\n        match infer_var {\n            TyOrConstInferVar::Ty(v) => {\n                use self::type_variable::TypeVariableValue;\n\n                // If `inlined_probe` returns a `Known` value, it never equals\n                // `ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(v))`.\n                match self.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().inlined_probe(v) {\n                    TypeVariableValue::Unknown { .. } => false,\n                    TypeVariableValue::Known { .. } => true,\n                }\n            }\n\n            TyOrConstInferVar::TyInt(v) => {\n                // If `inlined_probe_value` returns a value it's always a\n                // `ty::Int(_)` or `ty::UInt(_)`, which never matches a\n                // `ty::Infer(_)`.\n                self.inner.borrow_mut().int_unification_table().inlined_probe_value(v).is_known()\n            }\n\n            TyOrConstInferVar::TyFloat(v) => {\n                // If `probe_value` returns a value it's always a\n                // `ty::Float(_)`, which never matches a `ty::Infer(_)`.\n                //\n                // Not `inlined_probe_value(v)` because this call site is colder.\n                self.inner.borrow_mut().float_unification_table().probe_value(v).is_known()\n            }\n\n            TyOrConstInferVar::Const(v) => {\n                // If `probe_value` returns a `Known` value, it never equals\n                // `ty::ConstKind::Infer(ty::InferConst::Var(v))`.\n                //\n                // Not `inlined_probe_value(v)` because this call site is colder.\n                match self.inner.borrow_mut().const_unification_table().probe_value(v) {\n                    ConstVariableValue::Unknown { .. } => false,\n                    ConstVariableValue::Known { .. } => true,\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Attach a callback to be super_combine_tys};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::relate::{\n    Relate, RelateResult, TypeRelation, relate_args_invariantly, relate_args_with_variances,\n};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{self, DelayedSet, Ty, TyCtxt, TyVar};\nuse rustc_span::Span;\nuse tracing::{debug, instrument};\n\nuse crate::infer::BoundRegionConversionTime::HigherRankedType;\nuse crate::infer::relate::{PredicateEmittingRelation, StructurallyRelateAliases};\nuse crate::infer::{DefineOpaqueTypes, InferCtxt, SubregionOrigin, TypeTrace};\nuse crate::traits::{Obligation, PredicateObligations};\n\n/// Enforce that `a` is equal to or a subtype of `b`.\npub(crate) struct TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n\n    // Immutable fields\n    trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n    param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n    define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n\n    // Mutable fields.\n    ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    obligations: PredicateObligations<'tcx>,\n    /// The cache only tracks the `ambient_variance` as it's the\n    /// only field which is mutable and which meaningfully changes\n    /// the result when relating types.\n    ///\n    /// The cache does not track whether the state of the\n    /// `InferCtxt` has been changed or whether we've added any\n    /// obligations to `self.goals`. Whether a goal is added\n    /// once or multiple times is not really meaningful.\n    ///\n    /// Changes in the inference state may delay some type inference to\n    /// the next fulfillment loop. Given that this loop is already\n    /// necessary, this is also not a meaningful change. Consider\n    /// the following three relations:\n    /// ```text\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ?0 eq u32\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ```\n    /// Without a cache, the second `Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>` would eagerly\n    /// constrain `?1` to `u32`. When using the cache entry from the\n    /// first time we've related these types, this only happens when\n    /// later proving the `Subtype(?0, ?1)` goal from the first relation.\n    cache: DelayedSet<(ty::Variance, Ty<'tcx>, Ty<'tcx>)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'infcx, 'tcx> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    pub(crate) fn new(\n        infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n        trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n        ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    ) -> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n        assert!(!infcx.next_trait_solver);\n        TypeRelating {\n            infcx,\n            trace,\n            param_env,\n            define_opaque_types,\n            ambient_variance,\n            obligations: PredicateObligations::new(),\n            cache: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn into_obligations(self) -> PredicateObligations<'tcx> {\n        self.obligations\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> TypeRelation<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn cx(&self) -> TyCtxt<'tcx> {\n        self.infcx.tcx\n    }\n\n    fn relate_item_args(\n        &mut self,\n        item_def_id: rustc_hir::def_id::DefId,\n        a_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n        b_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>> {\n        if self.ambient_variance == ty::Invariant {\n            // Avoid fetching the variance if we are in an invariant\n            // context; no need, and it can induce dependency cycles\n            // (e.g., #41849).\n            relate_args_invariantly(self, a_arg, b_arg)\n        } else {\n            let tcx = self.cx();\n            let opt_variances = tcx.variances_of(item_def_id);\n            relate_args_with_variances(self, item_def_id, opt_variances, a_arg, b_arg, false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn relate_with_variance<T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>>(\n        &mut self,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        _info: ty::VarianceDiagInfo<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n        a: T,\n        b: T,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, T> {\n        let old_ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance;\n        self.ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance.xform(variance);\n        debug!(?self.ambient_variance, \"new ambient variance\");\n\n        let r = if self.ambient_variance == ty::Bivariant { Ok(a) } else { self.relate(a, b) };\n\n        self.ambient_variance = old_ambient_variance;\n        r\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn tys(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) -> RelateResult<'tcx, Ty<'tcx>> {\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        let infcx = self.infcx;\n        let a = infcx.shallow_resolve(a);\n        let b = infcx.shallow_resolve(b);\n\n        if self.cache.contains(&(self.ambient_variance, a, b)) {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match (a.kind(), b.kind()) {\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_id)), &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_id))) => {\n                match self.ambient_variance {\n                    ty::Covariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `A <: B` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                a,\n                                b,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Contravariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `B <: A` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n                                a_is_expected: false,\n                                a: b,\n                                b: a,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Invariant => {\n                        infcx.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().equate(a_id, b_id);\n                    }\n                    ty::Bivariant => {\n                        unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_vid)), _) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(self, true, a_vid, self.ambient_variance, b)?;\n            }\n            (_, &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(\n                    self,\n                    false,\n                    b_vid,\n                    self.ambient_variance.xform(ty::Contravariant),\n                    a,\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            (\n                &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: a_def_id, .. }),\n                &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: b_def_id, .. }),\n            ) if a_def_id == b_def_id => {\n                super_combine_tys(infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n\n            (&ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id, .. }), _)\n            | (_, &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id, .. }))\n                if self.define_opaque_types == DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes && def_id.is_local() =>\n            {\n                self.register_goals(infcx.handle_opaque_type(\n                    a,\n                    b,\n                    self.trace.cause.span,\n                    self.param_env(),\n                )?);\n            }\n\n            _ => {\n                super_combine_tys(infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        assert!(self.cache.insert((self.ambient_variance, a, b)));\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn regions(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Region<'tcx>> {\n        let origin = SubregionOrigin::Subtype(Box::new(self.trace.clone()));\n\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Subtype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('a: 'b) => SubRegion('b, 'a)\n            ty::Covariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, b, a);\n            }\n            // Suptype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('b: 'a) => SubRegion('a, 'b)\n            ty::Contravariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Invariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_eqregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn consts(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Const<'tcx>> {\n        super_combine_consts(self.infcx, self, a, b)\n    }\n\n    fn binders<T>(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n        b: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Binder<'tcx, T>>\n    where\n        T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        if a == b {\n            // Do nothing\n        } else if let Some(a) = a.no_bound_vars()\n            && let Some(b) = b.no_bound_vars()\n        {\n            self.relate(a, b)?;\n        } else {\n            let span = self.trace.cause.span;\n            let infcx = self.infcx;\n\n            match self.ambient_variance {\n                // Checks whether `for<..> sub <: for<..> sup` holds.\n                //\n                // For this to hold, **all** instantiations of the super type\n                // have to be a super type of **at least one** instantiation of\n                // the subtype.\n                //\n                // This is implemented by first entering a new universe.\n                // We then replace all bound variables in `sup` with placeholders,\n                // and all bound variables in `sub` with inference vars.\n                // We can then just relate the two resulting types as normal.\n                //\n                // Note: this is a subtle algorithm. For a full explanation, please see\n                // the [rustc dev guide][rd]\n                //\n                // [rd]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/borrow_check/region_inference/placeholders_and_universes.html\n                ty::Covariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Contravariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n\n                // When **equating** binders, we check that there is a 1-to-1\n                // correspondence between the bound vars in both types.\n                //\n                // We do so by separately instantiating one of the binders with\n                // placeholders and the other with inference variables and then\n                // equating the instantiated types.\n                //\n                // We want `for<..> A == for<..> B` -- therefore we want\n                // `exists<..> A == for<..> B` and `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                // Check if `exists<..> A == for<..> B`\n                ty::Invariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n\n                    // Check if `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Bivariant => {\n                    unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> PredicateEmittingRelation<InferCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn span(&self) -> Span {\n        self.trace.span()\n    }\n\n    fn param_env(&self) -> ty::ParamEnv<'tcx> {\n        self.param_env\n    }\n\n    fn structurally_relate_aliases(&self) -> StructurallyRelateAliases {\n        StructurallyRelateAliases::No\n    }\n\n    fn register_predicates(\n        &mut self,\n        preds: impl IntoIterator<Item: ty::Upcast<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>,\n    ) {\n        self.obligations.extend(preds.into_iter().map(|pred| {\n            Obligation::new(self.infcx.tcx, self.trace.cause.clone(), self.param_env, pred)\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_goals(&mut self, goals: impl IntoIterator<Item = Goal<'tcx, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>) {\n        self.obligations.extend(goals.into_iter().map(|goal| {\n            Obligation::new(\n                self.infcx.tcx,\n                self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                goal.param_env,\n                goal.predicate,\n            )\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_alias_relate_predicate(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) {\n        self.register_predicates([ty::Binder::dummy(match self.ambient_variance {\n            ty::Covariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            // a :> b is b <: a\n            ty::Contravariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                b.into(),\n                a.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            ty::Invariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n            ),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        })]);\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":138,"oldLines":10,"newStart":138,"newLines":10,"lines":["                             self.cx(),","                             self.trace.cause.clone(),"," super_combine_tys};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::relate::{\n    Relate, RelateResult, TypeRelation, relate_args_invariantly, relate_args_with_variances,\n};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{self, DelayedSet, Ty, TyCtxt, TyVar};\nuse rustc_span::Span;\nuse tracing::{debug, instrument};\n\nuse crate::infer::BoundRegionConversionTime::HigherRankedType;\nuse crate::infer::relate::{PredicateEmittingRelation, StructurallyRelateAliases};\nuse crate::infer::{DefineOpaqueTypes, InferCtxt, SubregionOrigin, TypeTrace};\nuse crate::traits::{Obligation, PredicateObligations};\n\n/// Enforce that `a` is equal to or a subtype of `b`.\npub(crate) struct TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n\n    // Immutable fields\n    trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n    param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n    define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n\n    // Mutable fields.\n    ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    obligations: PredicateObligations<'tcx>,\n    /// The cache only tracks the `ambient_variance` as it's the\n    /// only field which is mutable and which meaningfully changes\n    /// the result when relating types.\n    ///\n    /// The cache does not track whether the state of the\n    /// `InferCtxt` has been changed or whether we've added any\n    /// obligations to `self.goals`. Whether a goal is added\n    /// once or multiple times is not really meaningful.\n    ///\n    /// Changes in the inference state may delay some type inference to\n    /// the next fulfillment loop. Given that this loop is already\n    /// necessary, this is also not a meaningful change. Consider\n    /// the following three relations:\n    /// ```text\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ?0 eq u32\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ```\n    /// Without a cache, the second `Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>` would eagerly\n    /// constrain `?1` to `u32`. When using the cache entry from the\n    /// first time we've related these types, this only happens when\n    /// later proving the `Subtype(?0, ?1)` goal from the first relation.\n    cache: DelayedSet<(ty::Variance, Ty<'tcx>, Ty<'tcx>)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'infcx, 'tcx> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    pub(crate) fn new(\n        infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n        trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n        ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    ) -> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n        assert!(!infcx.next_trait_solver);\n        TypeRelating {\n            infcx,\n            trace,\n            param_env,\n            define_opaque_types,\n            ambient_variance,\n            obligations: PredicateObligations::new(),\n            cache: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn into_obligations(self) -> PredicateObligations<'tcx> {\n        self.obligations\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> TypeRelation<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn cx(&self) -> TyCtxt<'tcx> {\n        self.infcx.tcx\n    }\n\n    fn relate_item_args(\n        &mut self,\n        item_def_id: rustc_hir::def_id::DefId,\n        a_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n        b_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>> {\n        if self.ambient_variance == ty::Invariant {\n            // Avoid fetching the variance if we are in an invariant\n            // context; no need, and it can induce dependency cycles\n            // (e.g., #41849).\n            relate_args_invariantly(self, a_arg, b_arg)\n        } else {\n            let tcx = self.cx();\n            let opt_variances = tcx.variances_of(item_def_id);\n            relate_args_with_variances(self, item_def_id, opt_variances, a_arg, b_arg, false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn relate_with_variance<T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>>(\n        &mut self,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        _info: ty::VarianceDiagInfo<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n        a: T,\n        b: T,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, T> {\n        let old_ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance;\n        self.ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance.xform(variance);\n        debug!(?self.ambient_variance, \"new ambient variance\");\n\n        let r = if self.ambient_variance == ty::Bivariant { Ok(a) } else { self.relate(a, b) };\n\n        self.ambient_variance = old_ambient_variance;\n        r\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn tys(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) -> RelateResult<'tcx, Ty<'tcx>> {\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        let infcx = self.infcx;\n        let a = infcx.shallow_resolve(a);\n        let b = infcx.shallow_resolve(b);\n\n        if self.cache.contains(&(self.ambient_variance, a, b)) {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match (a.kind(), b.kind()) {\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_id)), &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_id))) => {\n                match self.ambient_variance {\n                    ty::Covariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `A <: B` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                sub_ty: a,\n                                super_ty: b,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Contravariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `B <: A` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n                                a_is_expected: false,\n                                a: b,\n                                b: a,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Invariant => {\n                        infcx.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().equate(a_id, b_id);\n                    }\n                    ty::Bivariant => {\n                        unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_vid)), _) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(self, true, a_vid, self.ambient_variance, b)?;\n            }\n            (_, &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(\n                    self,\n                    false,\n                    b_vid,\n                    self.ambient_variance.xform(ty::Contravariant),\n                    a,\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            (\n                &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: a_def_id, .. }),\n                &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: b_def_id, .. }),\n            ) if a_def_id == b_def_id => {\n                super_combine_tys(infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n\n            (&ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id, .. }), _)\n            | (_, &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id, .. }))\n                if self.define_opaque_types == DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes && def_id.is_local() =>\n            {\n                self.register_goals(infcx.handle_opaque_type(\n                    a,\n                    b,\n                    self.trace.cause.span,\n                    self.param_env(),\n                )?);\n            }\n\n            _ => {\n                super_combine_tys(infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        assert!(self.cache.insert((self.ambient_variance, a, b)));\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn regions(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Region<'tcx>> {\n        let origin = SubregionOrigin::Subtype(Box::new(self.trace.clone()));\n\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Subtype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('a: 'b) => SubRegion('b, 'a)\n            ty::Covariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, b, a);\n            }\n            // Suptype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('b: 'a) => SubRegion('a, 'b)\n            ty::Contravariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Invariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_eqregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn consts(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Const<'tcx>> {\n        super_combine_consts(self.infcx, self, a, b)\n    }\n\n    fn binders<T>(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n        b: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Binder<'tcx, T>>\n    where\n        T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        if a == b {\n            // Do nothing\n        } else if let Some(a) = a.no_bound_vars()\n            && let Some(b) = b.no_bound_vars()\n        {\n            self.relate(a, b)?;\n        } else {\n            let span = self.trace.cause.span;\n            let infcx = self.infcx;\n\n            match self.ambient_variance {\n                // Checks whether `for<..> sub <: for<..> sup` holds.\n                //\n                // For this to hold, **all** instantiations of the super type\n                // have to be a super type of **at least one** instantiation of\n                // the subtype.\n                //\n                // This is implemented by first entering a new universe.\n                // We then replace all bound variables in `sup` with placeholders,\n                // and all bound variables in `sub` with inference vars.\n                // We can then just relate the two resulting types as normal.\n                //\n                // Note: this is a subtle algorithm. For a full explanation, please see\n                // the [rustc dev guide][rd]\n                //\n                // [rd]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/borrow_check/region_inference/placeholders_and_universes.html\n                ty::Covariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Contravariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n\n                // When **equating** binders, we check that there is a 1-to-1\n                // correspondence between the bound vars in both types.\n                //\n                // We do so by separately instantiating one of the binders with\n                // placeholders and the other with inference variables and then\n                // equating the instantiated types.\n                //\n                // We want `for<..> A == for<..> B` -- therefore we want\n                // `exists<..> A == for<..> B` and `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                // Check if `exists<..> A == for<..> B`\n                ty::Invariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n\n                    // Check if `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Bivariant => {\n                    unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> PredicateEmittingRelation<InferCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn span(&self) -> Span {\n        self.trace.span()\n    }\n\n    fn param_env(&self) -> ty::ParamEnv<'tcx> {\n        self.param_env\n    }\n\n    fn structurally_relate_aliases(&self) -> StructurallyRelateAliases {\n        StructurallyRelateAliases::No\n    }\n\n    fn register_predicates(\n        &mut self,\n        preds: impl IntoIterator<Item: ty::Upcast<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>,\n    ) {\n        self.obligations.extend(preds.into_iter().map(|pred| {\n            Obligation::new(self.infcx.tcx, self.trace.cause.clone(), self.param_env, pred)\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_goals(&mut self, goals: impl IntoIterator<Item = Goal<'tcx, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>) {\n        self.obligations.extend(goals.into_iter().map(|goal| {\n            Obligation::new(\n                self.infcx.tcx,\n                self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                goal.param_env,\n                goal.predicate,\n            )\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_alias_relate_predicate(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) {\n        self.register_predicates([ty::Binder::dummy(match self.ambient_variance {\n            ty::Covariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            // a :> b is b <: a\n            ty::Contravariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                b.into(),\n                a.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            ty::Invariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n            ),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        })]);\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":152,"oldLines":10,"newStart":152,"newLines":10,"lines":["                             self.cx(),","                             self.trace.cause.clone(),"," The next-generation trait solver, currently still WIP.\n//!\n//! As a user of rust, you can use `-Znext-solver` to enable the new trait solver.\n//!\n//! As a developer of rustc, you shouldn't be using the new trait\n//! solver without asking the trait-system-refactor-initiative, but it can\n//! be enabled with `InferCtxtBuilder::with_next_trait_solver`. This will\n//! ensure that trait solving using that inference context will be routed\n//! to the new trait solver.\n//!\n//! opaque_types.is_empty()\n        && normalization_nested_goals.is_empty()\n}\n\nfn has_only_region_constraints<I: Interner>(response: ty::Canonical<I, Response<I>>) -> bool {\n    let self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self))]\n    fn compute_region_outlives_goal(\n        &mut self,\n        goal: Goal<I, ty::OutlivesPredicate<I, I::Region>>,\n    ) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        let ty::OutlivesPredicate(a, b) = goal.predicate;\n        self.register_region_outlives(a, b);\n (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                self.sub_unify_ty_vids_raw(a_vid, b_vid);\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n            }\n            _ => {\n                self.sub(goal.param_env, goal.predicate.a, goal.predicate.b)?;\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn compute_dyn_compatible_goal(&mut self, trait_def_id: I::TraitId) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        if self.cx().trait_is_dyn_compatible(trait_def_id) {\n            self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n        } else {\n            Err(NoSolution)\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self))]\n    fn compute_well_formed_goal(&mut self, goal: Goal<I, I::Term>) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        match self.well_formed_goals(goal.param_env, goal.predicate) {\n            Some(goals) => {\n                self.add_goals(GoalSource::Misc, goals);\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n            None => self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn compute_unstable_feature_goal(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: <I as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        symbol: <I as Interner>::Symbol,\n    ) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        if QueryResult<I> {\n        match ct.kind() {\n            ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => {\n                // We never return `NoSolution` here as `evaluate_const` emits an\n                // error itself when failing to evaluate, so emitting an additional fulfillment\n                // error in that case is unnecessary noise. This may change in the future once\n                // evaluation failures are allowed to impact selection, e.g. generic const\n                // expressions in impl headers or `where`-clauses.\n\n                // FIXME(generic_const_exprs): Implement handling for generic\n                // const expressions here.\n                if let Some(_normalized) = self.evaluate_const(param_env, uv) {\n                    self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n                } else {\n                    self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n                }\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(_) => {\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(_) | ty::ConstKind::Value(_) | ty::ConstKind::Error(_) => {\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n            // We can freely ICE here as:\n            // - `Param` gets replaced with a placeholder during canonicalization\n            // - `Bound` cannot exist as we don't have a binder around the self Type\n            // - `Expr` is part of `feature(generic_const_exprs)` and is not implemented yet\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(_) | ty::ConstKind::Bound(_, _) | ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => {\n                panic!(\"unexpected const kind: {:?}\", ct)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn compute_const_arg_has_type_goal(\n        &mut self,\n        goal: Goal<I, (I::Const, I::Ty)>,\n    ) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        let (ct, ty) = goal.predicate;\n        let ct = self.structurally_normalize_const(goal.param_env, ct)?;\n\n        let ct_ty = match ct.kind() {\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(_) => {\n self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => {\n                self.cx().type_of(uv.def.into()).instantiate(self.cx(), uv.args)\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => unimplemented!(\n                \"`feature(generic_const_exprs)` is not supported in the new trait solver\"\n            ),\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(_) => {\n                unreachable!(\"`ConstKind::Param` should have been canonicalized to `Placeholder`\")\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Bound(_, _) => panic!(\"escaping bound vars in {:?}\", ct),\n            ty::ConstKind::Value(cv) => cv.ty(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(placeholder) self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\nenum MergeCandidateInfo {\n    AlwaysApplicable(usize),\n    EqualResponse,\n}\n\nimpl<D, I> EvalCtxt<'_, D>\nwhere\n    D: SolverDelegate<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    /// Try to merge multiple possible ways to prove a goal, if that is not possible returns `None`.\n    ///\n    /// In this case we tend to flounder and return ambiguity by calling `[EvalCtxt::flounder]`.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn try_merge_candidates(\n        &mut self,\n        candidates: &[Candidate<I>],\n    ) -> Option<(CanonicalResponse<I>, MergeCandidateInfo)> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let always_applicable = candidates.iter().enumerate().find(|(_, candidate)| {\n            candidate.result.value.certainty == Certainty::Yes\n                && has_no_inference_or_external_constraints(candidate.result)\n        });\n        if let Some((i, c)) = always_applicable {\n            return Some((c.result, MergeCandidateInfo::AlwaysApplicable(i)));\n        }\n\n        let one: CanonicalResponse<I> = candidates[0].result;\n        if candidates[1..].iter().all(|candidate| candidate.result == one) {\n            return Some((one, MergeCandidateInfo::EqualResponse));\n        }\n\n        None\n    }\n\n    fn bail_with_ambiguity(&mut self, candidates: &[Candidate<I>]) -> CanonicalResponse<I> {\n        debug_assert!(candidates.len() > 1);\n        let (cause, opaque_types_jank) = candidates.iter().fold(\n            (MaybeCause::Ambiguity, OpaqueTypesJank::AllGood),\n            |(c, jank), candidates| {\n                // We pull down the certainty of `Certainty::Yes` to ambiguity when combining\n                // these responses, b/c we're combining more than one response and this we\n                // don't know which one applies.\n                match candidates.result.value.certainty {\n                    Certainty::Yes => (c, jank),\n                    Certainty::Maybe { cause, opaque_types_jank } => {\n                        (c.or(cause), jank.or(opaque_types_jank))\n                    }\n                }\n            },\n        );\n        self.make_ambiguous_response_no_constraints(cause, opaque_types_jank)\n    }\n\n    /// If we fail to merge responses we flounder and return overflow or ambiguity.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn flounder(&mut self, candidates: &[Candidate<I>]) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return Err(NoSolution);\n        } else {\n            Ok(self.bail_with_ambiguity(candidates))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Normalize a type for when it is structurally matched on.\n    ///\n    /// This function is necessary in nearly all cases before matching on a type.\n    /// Not doing so is likely to be incomplete and therefore unsound during\n    /// coherence.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self, param_env), ret)]\n    fn structurally_normalize_ty(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        ty: I::Ty,\n    ) -> Result<I::Ty, NoSolution> {\n        self.structurally_normalize_term(param_env, ty.into()).map(|term| term.expect_ty())\n    }\n\n    /// Normalize a const for when it is structurally matched on, or more likely\n    /// when it needs `.try_to_*` called on it (e.g. to turn it into coherence.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self, param_env), ret)]\n    fn structurally_normalize_const(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        ct: I::Const,\n    ) -> Result<I::Const, NoSolution> {\n        self.structurally_normalize_term(param_env, ct.into()).map(|term| term.expect_const())\n    }\n\n    /// Normalize a term for when it is structurally matched on.\n    ///\n    /// This function is necessary in nearly all cases before matching on a ty/const.\n    /// Not doing so is likely to be incomplete and therefore unsound during coherence.\n    fn structurally_normalize_term(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        term: I::Term,\n    ) -> Result<I::Term, NoSolution> {\n        if let Some(_) = term.to_alias_term() {\n            let normalized_term = self.next_term_infer_of_kind(term);\n            let alias_relate_goal = Goal::new(\n                self.cx(),\n                param_env,\n                ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                    term,\n                    normalized_term,\n                    ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n                ),\n            );\n            // We normalize the self type to be able to relate it with\n            // types from candidates.\n            self.add_goal(GoalSource::TypeRelating, alias_relate_goal);\n            self.try_evaluate_added_goals()?;\n            Ok(self.resolve_vars_if_possible(normalized_term))\n        } else {\n            Ok(term)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn opaque_type_is_rigid(&self, def_id: I::DefId) -> bool {\n        match self.typing_mode() {\n            // Opaques are never rigid outside of analysis mode.\n            TypingMode::Coherence | TypingMode::PostAnalysis => false,\n            // During analysis, opaques are rigid unless they may be defined by\n            // the current body.\n            TypingMode::Analysis { defining_opaque_types_and_generators: non_rigid_opaques }\n            | TypingMode::Borrowck { defining_opaque_types: non_rigid_opaques }\n            | TypingMode::PostBorrowckAnalysis { defined_opaque_types: non_rigid_opaques } => {\n                !def_id.as_local().is_some_and(|def_id| non_rigid_opaques.contains(&def_id))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// The result of evaluating a goal.\n#[derive_where(Debug; I: Interner)]\npub struct GoalEvaluation<I: Interner> {\n    /// The goal we've evaluated. This is the input goal, but potentially with its\n    /// inference variables resolved. This never applies any inference constraints\n    /// from evaluating the goal.\n    ///\n    /// We rely on this to check whether root goals in HIR typeck had an unresolved\n    /// type inference variable in the input. We must not resolve this after evaluating\n    /// the goal as even if the inference variable has been resolved by evaluating the\n    /// goal itself, this goal may still end up failing due to region uniquification\n    /// later on.\n    ///\n    /// This is used as a minor optimization to avoid re-resolving inference variables\n    /// when reevaluating ambiguous goals. E.g. if we've got a goal `?x: Trait` with `?x`\n    /// already being constrained to `Vec<?y>`, then the first evaluation resolves it to\n    /// `Vec<?y>: Trait`. If this goal is still ambiguous and we later resolve `?y` to `u32`,\n    /// then reevaluating this goal now only needs to resolve `?y` while it would otherwise\n    /// have to resolve both `?x` and `?y`,\n    pub goal: Goal<I, I::Predicate>,\n    pub certainty: Certainty,\n    pub has_changed: HasChanged,\n    /// If the [`Certainty`] was `Maybe`, then keep track of whether the goal has changed\n    /// before rerunning it.\n    pub stalled_on: Option<GoalStalledOn<I>>,\n}\n\n/// The conditions that must change for a goal to warrant\n#[derive_where(Clone, Debug; I: Interner)]\npub struct GoalStalledOn<I: Interner> {\n    pub num_opaques: usize,\n    pub stalled_vars: Vec<I::GenericArg>,\n    pub sub_roots: Vec<TyVid>,\n    /// The certainty that will be returned on subsequent evaluations if this\n    /// goal remains stalled.\n    pub stalled_certainty: Certainty,\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":109,"oldLines":10,"newStart":109,"newLines":10,"lines":["     fn compute_coerce_goal(&mut self, goal: Goal<I, ty::CoercePredicate<I>>) -> QueryResult<I> {","         self.compute_subtype_goal(Goal {","             param_env: goal.param_env,","-            predicate: ty::SubtypePredicate {","+            predicate: ty::SubtypeRelation {","                 a_is_expected: false,","-                a: goal.predicate.a,","-                b: goal.predicate.b,","+                sub_ty: goal.predicate.sub_ty,","+                super_ty: goal.predicate.super_ty,","             },","         })","     }"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

The next-generation trait solver, currently still WIP.\n//!\n//! As a user of rust, you can use `-Znext-solver` to enable the new trait solver.\n//!\n//! As a developer of rustc, you shouldn't be using the new trait\n//! solver without asking the trait-system-refactor-initiative, but it can\n//! be enabled with `InferCtxtBuilder::with_next_trait_solver`. This will\n//! ensure that trait solving using that inference context will be routed\n//! to the new trait solver.\n//!\n//! For a high-level overview of how this solver works, check out the relevant\n//! section of the rustc-dev-guide.\n\nmod alias_relate;\nmod assembly;\nmod effect_goals;\nmod eval_ctxt;\npub mod inspect;\nmod normalizes_to;\nmod project_goals;\nmod search_graph;\nmod trait_goals;\n\nuse derive_where::derive_where;\nuse rustc_type_ir::inherent::*;\npub use rustc_type_ir::solve::*;\nuse rustc_type_ir::{self as ty, Interner, TyVid, TypingMode};\nuse tracing::instrument;\n\npub use self::eval_ctxt::{\n    EvalCtxt, GenerateProofTree, SolverDelegateEvalExt,\n    evaluate_root_goal_for_proof_tree_raw_provider,\n};\nuse crate::delegate::SolverDelegate;\nuse crate::solve::assembly::Candidate;\n\n/// How many fixpoint iterations we should attempt inside of the solver before bailing\n/// with overflow.\n///\n/// We previously used  `cx.recursion_limit().0.checked_ilog2().unwrap_or(0)` for this.\n/// However, it feels unlikely that uncreasing the recursion limit by a power of two\n/// to get one more iteration is every useful or desirable. We now instead used a constant\n/// here. Copy)]\npub enum HasChanged {\n    Yes,\n    No,\n}\n\n// FIXME(trait-system-refactor-initiative#117): we don't detect whether a response\n// ended up pulling down any universes.\nfn has_no_inference_or_external_constraints<I: Interner>(\n    response: ty::Canonical<I, Response<I>>,\n) -> bool {\n    let ExternalConstraintsData {\n        ref region_constraints,\n        ref self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self))]\n    fn compute_region_outlives_goal(\n        &mut self,\n (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                self.sub_unify_ty_vids_raw(a_vid, b_vid);\n self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n            None => self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS),\n        }\n QueryResult<I> {\n        match ct.kind() {\n            ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => {\n                // We never return `NoSolution` here as `evaluate_const` emits an\n                // This may change in the future once\n                // evaluation failures are allowed to impact selection, e.g. generic const\n                // expressions in impl headers or `where`-clauses.\n\n                // FIXME(generic_const_exprs): Implement handling for generic\n                // const expressions here.\n                if let Some(_normalized) = self.evaluate_const(param_env, uv) self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n                }\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(_) => {\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(_) | ty::ConstKind::Value(_) | ty::ConstKind::Error(_) => {\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n            // We can freely ICE here as:\n            // - `Param` gets replaced with a placeholder during canonicalization\n            // - `Bound` cannot exist as we don't have a binder around the self Type\n            // - `Expr` is part of `feature(generic_const_exprs)` and is not implemented yet\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(_) | ty::ConstKind::Bound(_, _) | ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => {\n                panic!(\"unexpected const kind: {:?}\", ct)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn compute_const_arg_has_type_goal(\n        &mut self,\n        goal: Goal<I, (I::Const, I::Ty)>,\n    ) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        let (ct, ty) = goal.predicate;\n        let ct = self.structurally_normalize_const(goal.param_env, ct)?;\n\n        let ct_ty = match ct.kind() {\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(_) => {\n self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => {\n                self.cx().type_of(uv.def.into()).instantiate(self.cx(), uv.args)\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => unimplemented!(\n                \"`feature(generic_const_exprs)` is not supported in the new trait solver\"\n            ),\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(_) => {\n                unreachable!(\"`ConstKind::Param` should have been canonicalized to `Placeholder`\")\n self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\nenum MergeCandidateInfo {\n    AlwaysApplicable(usize),\n    EqualResponse,\n}\n\nimpl<D, I> EvalCtxt<'_, D>\nwhere\n    D: SolverDelegate<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    /// Try to merge multiple possible ways to prove a goal, if that is not possible returns `None`.\n    ///\n    /// In this case we tend to flounder and return ambiguity by calling `[EvalCtxt::flounder]`.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn try_merge_candidates(\n        &mut self,\n        candidates: &[Candidate<I>],\n    ) -> Option<(CanonicalResponse<I>, MergeCandidateInfo)> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let always_applicable = candidates.iter().enumerate().find(|(_, candidate)| {\n            candidate.result.value.certainty == Certainty::Yes\n                && has_no_inference_or_external_constraints(candidate.result)\n        });\n        if let Some((i, c)) = always_applicable {\n            return Some((c.result, MergeCandidateInfo::AlwaysApplicable(i)));\n        }\n\n        let one: CanonicalResponse<I> = candidates[0].result;\n        if candidates[1..].iter().all(|candidate| candidate.result == one) {\n            return Some((one, MergeCandidateInfo::EqualResponse));\n        }\n\n        None\n    }\n\n    fn bail_with_ambiguity(&mut self, candidates: &[Candidate<I>]) -> CanonicalResponse<I> {\n        debug_assert!(candidates.len() > 1);\n        let (cause, opaque_types_jank) = candidates.iter().fold(\n            (MaybeCause::Ambiguity, OpaqueTypesJank::AllGood),\n            |(c, jank), candidates| {\n                // We pull down the certainty of `Certainty::Yes` to ambiguity when combining\n                // these responses, b/c we're combining more than one response and this we\n                // don't know which one applies.\n                match candidates.result.value.certainty {\n                    Certainty::Yes => (c, jank),\n                    Certainty::Maybe { cause, opaque_types_jank } => {\n                        (c.or(cause), jank.or(opaque_types_jank))\n                    }\n                }\n            },\n        );\n        self.make_ambiguous_response_no_constraints(cause, opaque_types_jank)\n    }\n\n    /// If we fail to merge responses we flounder and return overflow or ambiguity.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn flounder(&mut self, candidates: &[Candidate<I>]) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return Err(NoSolution);\n        } else {\n            Ok(self.bail_with_ambiguity(candidates))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Normalize a type for when it is structurally matched on.\n    ///\n    /// This function is necessary in nearly all cases before matching on a type.\n    /// Not doing so is likely to be incomplete and therefore unsound during\n    /// coherence.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self, param_env), ret)]\n    fn structurally_normalize_ty(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        ty: I::Ty,\n    ) -> Result<I::Ty, NoSolution> {\n        self.structurally_normalize_term(param_env, ty.into()).map(|term| term.expect_ty())\n    }\n\n    /// Normalize a const for when it is structurally matched on, or more likely\n    /// when it needs `.try_to_*` called on it (e.g. to turn it into Result<I::Const, NoSolution> {\n        self.structurally_normalize_term(param_env, ct.into()).map(|term| term.expect_const())\n    }\n\n    /// Normalize a term for when it is structurally matched on.\n    ///\n    /// This function is necessary in nearly all cases before matching on a ty/const.\n    /// Not doing so is likely to be incomplete and therefore unsound during coherence.\n    fn structurally_normalize_term(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        term: I::Term,\n    ) -> Result<I::Term, NoSolution> {\n        if let Some(_) = term.to_alias_term() {\n ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                    term,\n                    normalized_term,\n                    ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n                ),\n            );\n self.try_evaluate_added_goals()?;\n            Ok(self.resolve_vars_if_possible(normalized_term))\n        } else {\n            Ok(term)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn opaque_type_is_rigid(&self, def_id: I::DefId) -> bool {\n        match self.typing_mode() {\n            // Opaques are never rigid outside of analysis mode.\n            TypingMode::Coherence | TypingMode::PostAnalysis => false,\n            // During analysis, opaques are rigid unless they may be defined by\n            // the current body.\n            TypingMode::Analysis { defining_opaque_types_and_generators: non_rigid_opaques }\n            | TypingMode::Borrowck { defining_opaque_types: non_rigid_opaques }\n            | TypingMode::PostBorrowckAnalysis { defined_opaque_types: non_rigid_opaques } => {\n                !def_id.as_local().is_some_and(|def_id| non_rigid_opaques.contains(&def_id))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// The result of evaluating a goal.\n#[derive_where(Debug; I: Interner)]\npub struct GoalEvaluation<I: Interner> {\n    /// The goal we've evaluated. This is the input goal, but potentially with its\n    /// inference variables resolved. This never applies any inference constraints\n    /// from evaluating the goal.\n    ///\n    /// We rely on this to check whether root goals in HIR typeck had an unresolved\n    /// type inference variable in the input. We must not resolve this after evaluating\n    /// the goal as even if the inference variable has been resolved by evaluating the\n    /// goal itself, this goal may still end up failing due to region uniquification\n    /// later on.\n    ///\n    /// This is used as a minor optimization to avoid re-resolving inference variables\n    /// when reevaluating ambiguous goals. E.g. if we've got a goal `?x: Trait` with `?x`\n    /// already being constrained to `Vec<?y>`, then the first evaluation resolves it to\n    /// `Vec<?y>: Trait`. If this goal is still ambiguous and we later resolve `?y` to `u32`,\n    /// then reevaluating this goal now only needs to resolve `?y` while it would otherwise\n    /// have to resolve both `?x` and `?y`,\n    pub goal: Goal<I, I::Predicate>,\n    pub certainty: Certainty,\n    pub has_changed: HasChanged,\n    /// If the [`Certainty`] was `Maybe`, then keep track of whether the goal has changed\n    /// before rerunning it.\n    pub stalled_on: Option<GoalStalledOn<I>>,\n}\n\n/// The conditions that must change for a goal to warrant\n#[derive_where(Clone, Debug; ty, InferCtxtLike, Interner};\n\npub trait RelateExt: InferCtxtLike {\n    fn relate<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    > {\n        let mut relate =\n            SolverRelating::new(self,       &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    > {\n        let mut relate = SolverRelating::new(\n            self,\n            StructurallyRelateAliases::Yes,\n            ty::Invariant,\n            param_env,\n            span,\n        );\n        relate.relate(lhs, rhs)?;\n        Ok(relate.goals)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Enforce that `a` is equal to or a subtype of `b`.\npub struct SolverRelating<'infcx, Infcx, I: Interner> {\n    infcx: &'infcx Infcx,\n    // Immutable fields.\n    structurally_relate_aliases: StructurallyRelateAliases,\n    param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n    span: I::Span,\n    // Mutable fields.\n    ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    goals: Vec<Goal<I, I::Predicate>>,\n    /// The cache only tracks the `ambient_variance` as it's the\n    /// only field which is mutable and which meaningfully changes\n    /// the result when relating types.\n    ///\n    /// The cache does not track whether the state of the\n    /// `Infcx` has been changed or whether we've added any\n    /// goals to `self.goals`. Whether a goal is added once or multiple\n    /// times is not really meaningful.\n    ///\n    /// Changes in the inference state may delay some type inference to\n    /// the next fulfillment loop. Given that this loop is already\n    /// necessary, this is also not a meaningful change. Consider\n    /// the following three relations:\n    /// ```text\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ?0 eq u32\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ```\n    /// Without a cache, the second `Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>` would eagerly\n    /// constrain `?1` to `u32`. When using the cache entry from the\n    /// first time we've related these types, this only happens when\n    /// later proving the `Subtype(?0, ?1)` goal from the first relation.\n    cache: DelayedSet<(ty::Variance, I::Ty, I::Ty)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'infcx, Infcx, I> SolverRelating<'infcx, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    pub fn new(\n        infcx: &'infcx Infcx,\n        structurally_relate_aliases: StructurallyRelateAliases,\n        ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        span: I::Span,\n    ) -> Self {\n        SolverRelating {\n            infcx,\n            structurally_relate_aliases,\n            span,\n            ambient_variance,\n            param_env,\n            goals: vec![],\n            cache: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<Infcx, I> TypeRelation<I> for SolverRelating<'_, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    fn cx(&self) -> I {\n        self.infcx.cx()\n    }\n\n    fn relate_item_args(\n        &mut self,\n        item_def_id: I::DefId,\n        a_arg: I::GenericArgs,\n        b_arg: I::GenericArgs,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, I::GenericArgs> {\n        if self.ambient_variance == ty::Invariant {\n            // Avoid fetching the variance if we are in an invariant\n            // context; no need, and it can induce dependency cycles\n            // (e.g., #41849).\n            relate_args_invariantly(self, a_arg, b_arg)\n        } else {\n            let tcx = self.cx();\n            let opt_variances = tcx.variances_of(item_def_id);\n            relate_args_with_variances(self, item_def_id, opt_variances, a_arg, b_arg, false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn relate_with_variance<T: Relate<I>>(\n        &mut self,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        _info: VarianceDiagInfo<I>,\n        a: T,\n        b: T,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, T> {\n        let old_ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance;\n        self.ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance.xform(variance);\n        debug!(?self.ambient_variance, \"new ambient variance\");\n\n        let r = if self.ambient_variance == ty::Bivariant { Ok(a) } else { self.relate(a, b) };\n\n        self.ambient_variance = old_ambient_variance;\n        r\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn tys(&mut self, a: I::Ty, b: I::Ty) -> RelateResult<I, I::Ty> {\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        let infcx = self.infcx;\n        let a = infcx.shallow_resolve(a);\n        let b = infcx.shallow_resolve(b);\n\n        if self.cache.contains(&(self.ambient_variance, a, b)) {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match (a.kind(), b.kind()) {\n            (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_id)), ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_id))) => {\n                match self.ambient_variance {\n                    ty::Covariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `A <: B` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.goals.push(Goal::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                a,\n                                b,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Contravariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `B <: A` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.goals.push(Goal::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n                                a_is_expected: false,\n                                a: b,\n                                b: a,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Invariant => {\n                        infcx.equate_ty_vids_raw(a_id, b_id);\n                    }\n                    ty::Bivariant => {\n                        unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), _) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var_raw(self, true, a_vid, self.ambient_variance, b)?;\n            }\n            (_, ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var_raw(\n                    self,\n                    false,\n                    b_vid,\n                    self.ambient_variance.xform(ty::Contravariant),\n                    a,\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            _ => {\n                super_combine_tys(self.infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        assert!(self.cache.insert((self.ambient_variance, a, b)));\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn regions(&mut self, a: I::Region, b: I::Region) -> RelateResult<I, I::Region> {\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Subtype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('a: 'b) => SubRegion('b, 'a)\n            ty::Covariant => self.infcx.sub_regions(b, a, self.span),\n            // Suptype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('b: 'a) => SubRegion('a, 'b)\n            ty::Contravariant => self.infcx.sub_regions(a, b, self.span),\n            ty::Invariant => self.infcx.equate_regions(a, b, self.span),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn consts(&mut self, a: I::Const, b: I::Const) -> RelateResult<I, I::Const> {\n        super_combine_consts(self.infcx, self, a, b)\n    }\n\n    fn binders<T>(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Binder<I, T>,\n        b: ty::Binder<I, T>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, ty::Binder<I, T>>\n    where\n        T: Relate<I>,\n    {\n        // If they're equal, then short-circuit.\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        // If they have no bound vars, relate normally.\n        if let Some(a_inner) = a.no_bound_vars()\n            && let Some(b_inner) = b.no_bound_vars()\n        {\n            self.relate(a_inner, b_inner)?;\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Checks whether `for<..> sub <: for<..> sup` holds.\n            //\n            // For this to hold, **all** instantiations of the super type\n            // have to be a super type of **at least one** instantiation of\n            // the subtype.\n            //\n            // This is implemented by first entering a new universe.\n            // We then replace all bound variables in `sup` with placeholders,\n            // and all bound variables in `sub` with inference vars.\n            // We can then just relate the two resulting types as normal.\n            //\n            // Note: this is a subtle algorithm. For a full explanation, please see\n            // the [rustc dev guide][rd]\n            //\n            // [rd]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/borrow_check/region_inference/placeholders_and_universes.html\n            ty::Covariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                    let a = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(a);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n            ty::Contravariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                    let b = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(b);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n\n            // When **equating** binders, we check that there is a 1-to-1\n            // correspondence between the bound vars in both types.\n            //\n            // We do so by separately instantiating one of the binders with\n            // placeholders and the other with inference variables and then\n            // equating the instantiated types.\n            //\n            // We want `for<..> A == for<..> B` -- therefore we want\n            // `exists<..> A == for<..> B` and `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n            // Check if `exists<..> A == for<..> B`\n            ty::Invariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                    let a = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(a);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n\n                // Check if `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                    let b = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(b);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<Infcx, I> PredicateEmittingRelation<Infcx> for SolverRelating<'_, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    fn span(&self) -> I::Span {\n        Span::dummy()\n    }\n\n    fn param_env(&self) -> I::ParamEnv {\n        self.param_env\n    }\n\n    fn structurally_relate_aliases(&self) -> StructurallyRelateAliases {\n        self.structurally_relate_aliases\n    }\n\n    fn register_predicates(\n        &mut self,\n        obligations: impl IntoIterator<Item: ty::Upcast<I, I::Predicate>>,\n    ) {\n        self.goals.extend(\n            obligations.into_iter().map(|pred| Goal::new(self.infcx.cx(), self.param_env, pred)),\n        );\n    }\n\n    fn register_goals(&mut self, obligations: impl IntoIterator<Item = Goal<I, I::Predicate>>) {\n        self.goals.extend(obligations);\n    }\n\n    fn register_alias_relate_predicate(&mut self, a: I::Ty, b: I::Ty) {\n        self.register_predicates([ty::Binder::dummy(match self.ambient_variance {\n            ty::Covariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            // a :> b is b <: a\n            ty::Contravariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                b.into(),\n                a.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            ty::Invariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n            ),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        })]);\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":197,"oldLines":10,"newStart":197,"newLines":10,"lines":["                         self.goals.push(Goal::new(","
