      &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    > {\n        let mut relate = SolverRelating::new(\n            self,\n            StructurallyRelateAliases::Yes,\n            ty::Invariant,\n            param_env,\n            span,\n        );\n        relate.relate(lhs, rhs)?;\n        Ok(relate.goals)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Enforce that `a` is equal to or a subtype of `b`.\npub struct SolverRelating<'infcx, Infcx, I: Interner> {\n    infcx: &'infcx Infcx,\n    // Immutable fields.\n    structurally_relate_aliases: StructurallyRelateAliases,\n    param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n    span: I::Span,\n    // Mutable fields.\n    ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    goals: Vec<Goal<I, I::Predicate>>,\n    /// The cache only tracks the `ambient_variance` as it's the\n    /// only field which is mutable and which meaningfully changes\n    /// the result when relating types.\n    ///\n    /// The cache does not track whether the state of the\n    /// `Infcx` has been changed or whether we've added any\n    /// goals to `self.goals`. Whether a goal is added once or multiple\n    /// times is not really meaningful.\n    ///\n    /// Changes in the inference state may delay some type inference to\n    /// the next fulfillment loop. Given that this loop is already\n    /// necessary, this is also not a meaningful change. Consider\n    /// the following three relations:\n    /// ```text\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ?0 eq u32\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ```\n    /// Without a cache, the second `Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>` would eagerly\n    /// constrain `?1` to `u32`. When using the cache entry from the\n    /// first time we've related these types, this only happens when\n    /// later proving the `Subtype(?0, ?1)` goal from the first relation.\n    cache: DelayedSet<(ty::Variance, I::Ty, I::Ty)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'infcx, Infcx, I> SolverRelating<'infcx, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    pub fn new(\n        infcx: &'infcx Infcx,\n        structurally_relate_aliases: StructurallyRelateAliases,\n        ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        span: I::Span,\n    ) -> Self {\n        SolverRelating {\n            infcx,\n            structurally_relate_aliases,\n            span,\n            ambient_variance,\n            param_env,\n            goals: vec![],\n            cache: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<Infcx, I> TypeRelation<I> for SolverRelating<'_, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    fn cx(&self) -> I {\n        self.infcx.cx()\n    }\n\n    fn relate_item_args(\n        &mut self,\n        item_def_id: I::DefId,\n        a_arg: I::GenericArgs,\n        b_arg: I::GenericArgs,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, I::GenericArgs> {\n        if self.ambient_variance == ty::Invariant {\n            // Avoid fetching the variance if we are in an invariant\n            // context; no need, and it can induce dependency cycles\n            // (e.g., #41849).\n            relate_args_invariantly(self, a_arg, b_arg)\n        } else {\n            let tcx = self.cx();\n            let opt_variances = tcx.variances_of(item_def_id);\n            relate_args_with_variances(self, item_def_id, opt_variances, a_arg, b_arg, false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn relate_with_variance<T: Relate<I>>(\n        &mut self,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        _info: VarianceDiagInfo<I>,\n        a: T,\n        b: T,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, T> {\n        let old_ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance;\n        self.ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance.xform(variance);\n        debug!(?self.ambient_variance, \"new ambient variance\");\n\n        let r = if self.ambient_variance == ty::Bivariant { Ok(a) } else { self.relate(a, b) };\n\n        self.ambient_variance = old_ambient_variance;\n        r\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn tys(&mut self, a: I::Ty, b: I::Ty) -> RelateResult<I, I::Ty> {\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        let infcx = self.infcx;\n        let a = infcx.shallow_resolve(a);\n        let b = infcx.shallow_resolve(b);\n\n        if self.cache.contains(&(self.ambient_variance, a, b)) {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match (a.kind(), b.kind()) {\n            (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_id)), ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_id))) => {\n                match self.ambient_variance {\n                    ty::Covariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `A <: B` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.goals.push(Goal::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                sub_ty: a,\n                                super_ty: b,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Contravariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `B <: A` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.goals.push(Goal::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n                                a_is_expected: false,\n                                a: b,\n                                b: a,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Invariant => {\n                        infcx.equate_ty_vids_raw(a_id, b_id);\n                    }\n                    ty::Bivariant => {\n                        unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), _) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var_raw(self, true, a_vid, self.ambient_variance, b)?;\n            }\n            (_, ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var_raw(\n                    self,\n                    false,\n                    b_vid,\n                    self.ambient_variance.xform(ty::Contravariant),\n                    a,\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            _ => {\n                super_combine_tys(self.infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        assert!(self.cache.insert((self.ambient_variance, a, b)));\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn regions(&mut self, a: I::Region, b: I::Region) -> RelateResult<I, I::Region> {\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Subtype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('a: 'b) => SubRegion('b, 'a)\n            ty::Covariant => self.infcx.sub_regions(b, a, self.span),\n            // Suptype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('b: 'a) => SubRegion('a, 'b)\n            ty::Contravariant => self.infcx.sub_regions(a, b, self.span),\n            ty::Invariant => self.infcx.equate_regions(a, b, self.span),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn consts(&mut self, a: I::Const, b: I::Const) -> RelateResult<I, I::Const> {\n        super_combine_consts(self.infcx, self, a, b)\n    }\n\n    fn binders<T>(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Binder<I, T>,\n        b: ty::Binder<I, T>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, ty::Binder<I, T>>\n    where\n        T: Relate<I>,\n    {\n        // If they're equal, then short-circuit.\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        // If they have no bound vars, relate normally.\n        if let Some(a_inner) = a.no_bound_vars()\n            && let Some(b_inner) = b.no_bound_vars()\n        {\n            self.relate(a_inner, b_inner)?;\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Checks whether `for<..> sub <: for<..> sup` holds.\n            //\n            // For this to hold, **all** instantiations of the super type\n            // have to be a super type of **at least one** instantiation of\n            // the subtype.\n            //\n            // This is implemented by first entering a new universe.\n            // We then replace all bound variables in `sup` with placeholders,\n            // and all bound variables in `sub` with inference vars.\n            // We can then just relate the two resulting types as normal.\n            //\n            // Note: this is a subtle algorithm. For a full explanation, please see\n            // the [rustc dev guide][rd]\n            //\n            // [rd]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/borrow_check/region_inference/placeholders_and_universes.html\n            ty::Covariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                    let a = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(a);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n            ty::Contravariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                    let b = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(b);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n\n            // When **equating** binders, we check that there is a 1-to-1\n            // correspondence between the bound vars in both types.\n            //\n            // We do so by separately instantiating one of the binders with\n            // placeholders and the other with inference variables and then\n            // equating the instantiated types.\n            //\n            // We want `for<..> A == for<..> B` -- therefore we want\n            // `exists<..> A == for<..> B` and `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n            // Check if `exists<..> A == for<..> B`\n            ty::Invariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                    let a = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(a);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n\n                // Check if `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                    let b = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(b);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<Infcx, I> PredicateEmittingRelation<Infcx> for SolverRelating<'_, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    fn span(&self) -> I::Span {\n        Span::dummy()\n    }\n\n    fn param_env(&self) -> I::ParamEnv {\n        self.param_env\n    }\n\n    fn structurally_relate_aliases(&self) -> StructurallyRelateAliases {\n        self.structurally_relate_aliases\n    }\n\n    fn register_predicates(\n        &mut self,\n        obligations: impl IntoIterator<Item: ty::Upcast<I, I::Predicate>>,\n    ) {\n        self.goals.extend(\n            obligations.into_iter().map(|pred| Goal::new(self.infcx.cx(), self.param_env, pred)),\n        );\n    }\n\n    fn register_goals(&mut self, obligations: impl IntoIterator<Item = Goal<I, I::Predicate>>) {\n        self.goals.extend(obligations);\n    }\n\n    fn register_alias_relate_predicate(&mut self, a: I::Ty, b: I::Ty) {\n        self.register_predicates([ty::Binder::dummy(match self.ambient_variance {\n            ty::Covariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            // a :> b is b <: a\n            ty::Contravariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                b.into(),\n                a.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            ty::Invariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n            ),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        })]);\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":210,"oldLines":10,"newStart":210,"newLines":10,"lines":["                         self.goals.push(Goal::new("," rustc_data_structures::graph::vec_graph::VecGraph;\nuse rustc_data_structures::graph::{self};\nuse rustc_data_structures::unord::{UnordMap, UnordSet};\nuse rustc_hir as hir;\nuse rustc_hir::HirId;\nuse rustc_hir::def::{DefKind, Res};\nuse rustc_hir::def_id::DefId;\nuse rustc_hir::intravisit::{InferKind, Visitor};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{self, Ty, TyCtxt, TypeSuperVisitable, TypeVisitable};\nuse rustc_session::lint;\nuse rustc_span::def_id::LocalDefId;\nuse rustc_span::{DUMMY_SP, Span};\nuse rustc_trait_selection::traits::{ObligationCause, ObligationCtxt};\nuse tracing::debug;\n\nuse crate::{FnCtxt, errors};\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone)]\npub(crate) enum DivergingFallbackBehavior {\n    /// Always fallback to `()` (aka \"always spontaneous decay\")\n    ToUnit,\n    /// Always fallback to `!` (which should be equivalent to never falling back + not making\n    /// never-to-any coercions unless necessary)\n    ToNever,\n    /// Don't fallback at all\n    NoFallback,\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> FnCtxt<'_, 'tcx> {\n    /// Performs type inference fallback, setting [`FnCtxt::diverging_fallback_has_occurred`]\n    /// if the never type fallback has occurred.\n    pub(super) fn type_inference_fallback(&self) {\n        debug!(\n            \"type-inference-fallback start obligations: {:#?}\",\n            self.fulfillment_cx.borrow_mut().pending_obligations()\n        );\n\n        // All type checking constraints were added, try to fallback unsolved variables.\n        self.select_obligations_where_possible(|_| {});\n\n        debug!(\n            \"type-inference-fallback post selection obligations: {:#?}\",\n            self.fulfillment_cx.borrow_mut().pending_obligations()\n        );\n\n        let fallback_occurred = self.fallback_types();\n\n        if fallback_occurred {\n            // if fallback occurred, previously stalled goals may make progress again\n            self.select_obligations_where_possible(|_| {});\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn fallback_types(&self) -> bool {\n        // Check if we have any unresolved variables. If not, no need for fallback.\n        let unresolved_variables = self.unresolved_variables();\n\n        if unresolved_variables.is_empty() {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        let (diverging_fallback, diverging_fallback_ty) =\n            self.calculate_diverging_fallback(&unresolved_variables);\n\n        // We do fallback in two passes, to try to generate\n        // better error messages.\n        // The first time, we do *not* replace opaque types.\n        let mut fallback_occurred = false;\n        for ty in unresolved_variables {\n            debug!(\"unsolved_variable = {:?}\", ty);\n            fallback_occurred |=\n                self.fallback_if_possible(ty, &diverging_fallback, diverging_fallback_ty);\n        }\n\n        fallback_occurred\n    }\n\n    /// Tries to apply a fallback to `ty` if it is an unsolved variable.\n    ///\n    /// - Unconstrained ints are replaced with `i32`.\n    ///\n    /// - Unconstrained floats are replaced with `f64`.\n    ///\n    /// - Non-numerics may get replaced with `()` or `!`, depending on how they\n    ///   were categorized by [`Self::calculate_diverging_fallback`], crate's\n    ///   edition, and the setting of `#![rustc_never_type_options(fallback = ...)]`.\n    ///\n    /// Fallback becomes very dubious if we have encountered\n    /// type-checking errors. In that case, fallback to Error.\n    ///\n    /// Sets [`FnCtxt::diverging_fallback_has_occurred`] if never type fallback\n    /// is performed during this call.\n    fn fallback_if_possible(\n        &self,\n        ty: Ty<'tcx>,\n        diverging_fallback: &UnordSet<Ty<'tcx>>,\n        diverging_fallback_ty: Ty<'tcx>,\n    ) -> bool {\n        // Careful: we do NOT shallow-resolve `ty`. We know that `ty`\n        // is an unsolved variable, and we determine its fallback\n        // based solely on how it was created, not what other type\n        // variables it may have been unified with since then.\n        //\n        // The reason this matters is that other attempts at fallback\n        // may (in principle) conflict with this fallback, and we wish\n        // to generate a type error in that case. (However, this\n        // actually isn't true right now, because we're only using the\n        // builtin fallback rules. This would be true if we were using\n        // user-supplied fallbacks. But it's still useful to write the\n        // code to detect bugs.)\n        //\n        // (Note though that if we have a general type variable `?T`\n        // that is then unified with an integer type variable `?I`\n        // that ultimately never gets resolved to a special integral\n        // type, `?T` is not considered unsolved, but `?I` is. The\n        // same is true for float variables.)\n        let fallback = match ty.kind() {\n            _ if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() => Ty::new_error(self.tcx, e),\n            ty::Infer(ty::IntVar(_)) => self.tcx.types.i32,\n            ty::Infer(ty::FloatVar(_)) => self.tcx.types.f64,\n            _ if diverging_fallback.contains(&ty) => {\n                self.diverging_fallback_has_occurred.set(true);\n                diverging_fallback_ty\n            }\n            _ => return false,\n        };\n        debug!(\"fallback_if_possible(ty={:?}): defaulting to `{:?}`\", ty, fallback);\n\n        let span = ty.ty_vid().map_or(DUMMY_SP, |vid| self.infcx.type_var_origin(vid).span);\n        self.demand_eqtype(span, ty, fallback);\n        true\n    }\n\n    fn calculate_diverging_fallback(\n        &self,\n        unresolved_variables: &[Ty<'tcx>],\n    ) -> (UnordSet<Ty<'tcx>>, Ty<'tcx>) {\n        debug!(\"calculate_diverging_fallback({:?})\", unresolved_variables);\n\n        let diverging_fallback_ty = match self.diverging_fallback_behavior {\n            DivergingFallbackBehavior::ToUnit => self.tcx.types.unit,\n            DivergingFallbackBehavior::ToNever => self.tcx.types.never,\n            DivergingFallbackBehavior::NoFallback => {\n                // the type doesn't matter, since no fallback will occur\n                return (UnordSet::new(), self.tcx.types.unit);\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Construct a coercion graph where an edge `A -> B` indicates\n        // a type variable is that is coerced\n        let coercion_graph = self.create_coercion_graph();\n\n        // Extract the unsolved type inference variable vids; note that some\n        // unsolved variables are integer/float variables and are excluded.\n        let unsolved_vids = unresolved_variables.iter().filter_map(|ty| ty.ty_vid());\n\n        // Compute the diverging root vids D -- that is, the root vid of\n        // those type variables that (a) are the target of a coercion from\n        // a `!` type and (b) have not yet been solved.\n        //\n        // These variables are the ones that are targets for fallback to\n        // either `!` or `()`.\n        let diverging_roots: UnordSet<ty::TyVid> = self\n            .diverging_type_vars\n            .borrow()\n            .items()\n            .map(|&ty| self.shallow_resolve(ty))\n            .filter_map(|ty| ty.ty_vid())\n            .map(|vid| self.root_var(vid))\n            .collect();\n        debug!(\n            \"calculate_diverging_fallback: diverging_type_vars={:?}\",\n            self.diverging_type_vars.borrow()\n        );\n        debug!(\"calculate_diverging_fallback: diverging_roots={:?}\", diverging_roots);\n\n        // Find all type variables that are reachable from a diverging\n        // type variable. These will typically default to `!`, unless\n        // we find later that they are *also* reachable from some\n        // other type variable outside this set.\n        let mut diverging_vids = vec![];\n        for unsolved_vid in unsolved_vids {\n            let root_vid = self.root_var(unsolved_vid);\n            debug!(\n                \"calculate_diverging_fallback: unsolved_vid={:?} root_vid={:?} diverges={:?}\",\n                unsolved_vid,\n                root_vid,\n                diverging_roots.contains(&root_vid),\n            );\n            if diverging_roots.contains(&root_vid) {\n                diverging_vids.push(unsolved_vid);\n\n                debug!(\n                    \"calculate_diverging_fallback: root_vid={:?} reaches {:?}\",\n                    root_vid,\n                    graph::depth_first_search(&coercion_graph, root_vid).collect::<Vec<_>>()\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        debug!(\"obligations: {:#?}\", self.fulfillment_cx.borrow_mut().pending_obligations());\n\n        let mut diverging_fallback = UnordSet::with_capacity(diverging_vids.len());\n        let unsafe_infer_vars = OnceCell::new();\n\n        self.lint_obligations_broken_by_never_type_fallback_change(\n            &diverging_vids,\n            &coercion_graph,\n        );\n\n        for &diverging_vid in &diverging_vids {\n            let diverging_ty = Ty::new_var(self.tcx, diverging_vid);\n            let root_vid = self.root_var(diverging_vid);\n\n            self.lint_never_type_fallback_flowing_into_unsafe_code(\n                &unsafe_infer_vars,\n                &coercion_graph,\n                root_vid,\n            );\n\n            diverging_fallback.insert(diverging_ty);\n        }\n\n        (diverging_fallback, diverging_fallback_ty)\n    }\n\n    fn lint_never_type_fallback_flowing_into_unsafe_code(\n        &self,\n        unsafe_infer_vars: &OnceCell<UnordMap<ty::TyVid, (HirId, Span, UnsafeUseReason)>>,\n        coercion_graph: &VecGraph<ty::TyVid, true>,\n        root_vid: ty::TyVid,\n    ) {\n        let unsafe_infer_vars = unsafe_infer_vars.get_or_init(|| {\n            let unsafe_infer_vars = compute_unsafe_infer_vars(self, self.body_id);\n            debug!(?unsafe_infer_vars);\n            unsafe_infer_vars\n        });\n\n        let affected_unsafe_infer_vars =\n            graph::depth_first_search_as_undirected(&coercion_graph, root_vid)\n                .filter_map(|x| unsafe_infer_vars.get(&x).copied())\n                .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n\n        let sugg = self.try_to_suggest_annotations(&[root_vid], coercion_graph);\n\n        for (hir_id, span, reason) in affected_unsafe_infer_vars {\n            self.tcx.emit_node_span_lint(\n                lint::builtin::NEVER_TYPE_FALLBACK_FLOWING_INTO_UNSAFE,\n                hir_id,\n                span,\n                match reason {\n                    UnsafeUseReason::Call => {\n                        errors::NeverTypeFallbackFlowingIntoUnsafe::Call { sugg: sugg.clone() }\n                    }\n                    UnsafeUseReason::Method => {\n                        errors::NeverTypeFallbackFlowingIntoUnsafe::Method { sugg: sugg.clone() }\n                    }\n                    UnsafeUseReason::Path => {\n                        errors::NeverTypeFallbackFlowingIntoUnsafe::Path { sugg: sugg.clone() }\n                    }\n                    UnsafeUseReason::UnionField => {\n                        errors::NeverTypeFallbackFlowingIntoUnsafe::UnionField {\n                            sugg: sugg.clone(),\n                        }\n                    }\n                    UnsafeUseReason::Deref => {\n                        errors::NeverTypeFallbackFlowingIntoUnsafe::Deref { sugg: sugg.clone() }\n                    }\n                },\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn lint_obligations_broken_by_never_type_fallback_change(\n        &self,\n        diverging_vids: &[ty::TyVid],\n        coercions: &VecGraph<ty::TyVid, true>,\n    ) {\n        let DivergingFallbackBehavior::ToUnit = self.diverging_fallback_behavior else { return };\n\n        // Fallback happens if and only if there are diverging variables\n        if diverging_vids.is_empty() {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Returns errors which happen if fallback is set to `fallback`\n        let remaining_errors_if_fallback_to = |fallback| {\n            self.probe(|_| {\n                let obligations = self.fulfillment_cx.borrow().pending_obligations();\n                let ocx = ObligationCtxt::new_with_diagnostics(&self.infcx);\n                ocx.register_obligations(obligations.iter().cloned());\n\n                for &diverging_vid in diverging_vids {\n                    let diverging_ty = Ty::new_var(self.tcx, diverging_vid);\n\n                    ocx.eq(&ObligationCause::dummy(), self.param_env, diverging_ty, fallback)\n                        .expect(\"expected diverging var to be unconstrained\");\n                }\n\n                ocx.try_evaluate_obligations()\n            })\n        };\n\n        // If we have no errors with `fallback = ()`, but *do* have errors with `fallback = !`,\n        // then this code will be broken by the never type fallback change.\n        let unit_errors = remaining_errors_if_fallback_to(self.tcx.types.unit);\n        if unit_errors.is_empty()\n            && let mut never_errors = remaining_errors_if_fallback_to(self.tcx.types.never)\n            && let [never_error, ..] = never_errors.as_mut_slice()\n        {\n            self.adjust_fulfillment_error_for_expr_obligation(never_error);\n            let sugg = self.try_to_suggest_annotations(diverging_vids, coercions);\n            self.tcx.emit_node_span_lint(\n                lint::builtin::DEPENDENCY_ON_UNIT_NEVER_TYPE_FALLBACK,\n                self.tcx.local_def_id_to_hir_id(self.body_id),\n                self.tcx.def_span(self.body_id),\n                errors::DependencyOnUnitNeverTypeFallback {\n                    obligation_span: never_error.obligation.cause.span,\n                    obligation: never_error.obligation.predicate,\n                    sugg,\n                },\n            )\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns a graph whose nodes are (unresolved) inference variables and where\n    /// an edge `?A -> ?B` indicates that the variable `?A` is coerced to `?B`.\n    fn create_coercion_graph(&self) -> VecGraph<ty::TyVid, true> {\n        let pending_obligations = self.fulfillment_cx.borrow_mut().pending_obligations();\n        debug!(\"create_coercion_graph: pending_obligations={:?}\", pending_obligations);\n        let coercion_edges: Vec<(ty::TyVid, ty::TyVid)> = pending_obligations\n            .into_iter()\n            .filter_map(|obligation| {\n                // The predicates we are looking for look like `Coerce(?A -> ?B)`.\n                // They will have no bound variables.\n                obligation.predicate.kind().no_bound_vars()\n            })\n            .filter_map(|atom| {\n                // We consider both subtyping and coercion to imply 'flow' from\n                // some position in the code `a` to a different position `b`.\n                // This is then used to determine which variables interact with\n                // live code, and as such must fall back to `()` to preserve\n                // soundness.\n                //\n                // In practice currently the two ways that this happens is\n                // coercion and subtyping.\n                let (a, b) = match atom {\n                    ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => (a, b),\n                    ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected: _, a, b }) => {\n                        (a, b)\n                    }\n                    _ => return None,\n                };\n\n                let a_vid = self.root_vid(a)?;\n                let b_vid = self.root_vid(b)?;\n                Some((a_vid, b_vid))\n            })\n            .collect();\n        debug!(\"create_coercion_graph: coercion_edges={:?}\", coercion_edges);\n        let num_ty_vars = self.num_ty_vars();\n\n        VecGraph::new(num_ty_vars, coercion_edges)\n    }\n\n    /// If `ty` is an unresolved type variable, returns its root vid.\n    fn root_vid(&self, ty: Ty<'tcx>) -> Option<ty::TyVid> {\n        Some(self.root_var(self.shallow_resolve(ty).ty_vid()?))\n    }\n\n    /// Given a set of diverging vids and coercions, walk the HIR to gather a\n    /// set of suggestions which can be applied to preserve fallback to unit.\n    fn try_to_suggest_annotations(\n        &self,\n        diverging_vids: &[ty::TyVid],\n        coercions: &VecGraph<ty::TyVid, true>,\n    ) -> errors::SuggestAnnotations {\n        let body =\n            self.tcx.hir_maybe_body_owned_by(self.body_id).expect(\"body id must have an owner\");\n        // For each diverging var, look through the HIR for a place to give it\n        // a type annotation. We do this per var because we only really need one\n        // suggestion to influence a var to be `()`.\n        let suggestions = diverging_vids\n            .iter()\n            .copied()\n            .filter_map(|vid| {\n                let reachable_vids =\n                    graph::depth_first_search_as_undirected(coercions, vid).collect();\n                AnnotateUnitFallbackVisitor { reachable_vids, fcx: self }\n                    .visit_expr(body.value)\n                    .break_value()\n            })\n            .collect();\n        errors::SuggestAnnotations { suggestions }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Try to walk the HIR to find a place to insert a useful suggestion\n/// to preserve fallback to `()` in 2024.\nstruct AnnotateUnitFallbackVisitor<'a, 'tcx> {\n    reachable_vids: FxHashSet<ty::TyVid>,\n    fcx: &'a FnCtxt<'a, 'tcx>,\n}\nimpl<'tcx> AnnotateUnitFallbackVisitor<'_, 'tcx> {\n    // For a given path segment, if it's missing a turbofish, try to suggest adding\n    // one so we can constrain an argument to `()`. To keep the suggestion simple,\n    // we want to simply suggest `_` for all the other args. This (for now) only\n    // works when there are only type variables (and region variables, since we can\n    // elide them)...\n    fn suggest_for_segment(\n        &self,\n        arg_segment: &'tcx hir::PathSegment<'tcx>,\n        def_id: DefId,\n        id: HirId,\n    ) -> ControlFlow<errors::SuggestAnnotation> {\n        if arg_segment.args.is_none()\n            && let Some(all_args) = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().node_args_opt(id)\n            && let generics = self.fcx.tcx.generics_of(def_id)\n            && let args = all_args[generics.parent_count..].iter().zip(&generics.own_params)\n            // We can't turbofish consts :(\n            && args.clone().all(|(_, param)| matches!(param.kind, ty::GenericParamDefKind::Type { .. } | ty::GenericParamDefKind::Lifetime))\n        {\n            // We filter out APITs, which are not turbofished.\n            let non_apit_type_args = args.filter(|(_, param)| {\n                matches!(param.kind, ty::GenericParamDefKind::Type { synthetic: false, .. })\n            });\n            let n_tys = non_apit_type_args.clone().count();\n            for (idx, (arg, _)) in non_apit_type_args.enumerate() {\n                if let Some(ty) = arg.as_type()\n                    && let Some(vid) = self.fcx.root_vid(ty)\n                    && self.reachable_vids.contains(&vid)\n                {\n                    return ControlFlow::Break(errors::SuggestAnnotation::Turbo(\n                        arg_segment.ident.span.shrink_to_hi(),\n                        n_tys,\n                        idx,\n                    ));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        ControlFlow::Continue(())\n    }\n}\nimpl<'tcx> Visitor<'tcx> for AnnotateUnitFallbackVisitor<'_, 'tcx> {\n    type Result = ControlFlow<errors::SuggestAnnotation>;\n\n    fn visit_infer(\n        &mut self,\n        inf_id: HirId,\n        inf_span: Span,\n        _kind: InferKind<'tcx>,\n    ) -> Self::Result {\n        // Try to replace `_` with `()`.\n        if let Some(ty) = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().node_type_opt(inf_id)\n            && let Some(vid) = self.fcx.root_vid(ty)\n            && self.reachable_vids.contains(&vid)\n            && inf_span.can_be_used_for_suggestions()\n        {\n            return ControlFlow::Break(errors::SuggestAnnotation::Unit(inf_span));\n        }\n\n        ControlFlow::Continue(())\n    }\n\n    fn visit_qpath(\n        &mut self,\n        qpath: &'tcx rustc_hir::QPath<'tcx>,\n        id: HirId,\n        span: Span,\n    ) -> Self::Result {\n        let arg_segment = match qpath {\n            hir::QPath::Resolved(_, path) => {\n                path.segments.last().expect(\"paths should have a segment\")\n            }\n            hir::QPath::TypeRelative(_, segment) => segment,\n        };\n        // Alternatively, try to turbofish `::<_, (), _>`.\n        if let Some(def_id) = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().qpath_res(qpath, id).opt_def_id()\n            && span.can_be_used_for_suggestions()\n        {\n            self.suggest_for_segment(arg_segment, def_id, id)?;\n        }\n        hir::intravisit::walk_qpath(self, qpath, id)\n    }\n\n    fn visit_expr(&mut self, expr: &'tcx hir::Expr<'tcx>) -> Self::Result {\n        if let hir::ExprKind::Closure(&hir::Closure { body, .. })\n        | hir::ExprKind::ConstBlock(hir::ConstBlock { body, .. }) = expr.kind\n        {\n            self.visit_body(self.fcx.tcx.hir_body(body))?;\n        }\n\n        // Try to suggest adding an explicit qself `()` to a trait method path.\n        // i.e. changing `Default::default()` to `<() as Default>::default()`.\n        if let hir::ExprKind::Path(hir::QPath::Resolved(None, path)) = expr.kind\n            && let Res::Def(DefKind::AssocFn, def_id) = path.res\n            && self.fcx.tcx.trait_of_assoc(def_id).is_some()\n            && let Some(args) = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().node_args_opt(expr.hir_id)\n            && let self_ty = args.type_at(0)\n            && let Some(vid) = self.fcx.root_vid(self_ty)\n            && self.reachable_vids.contains(&vid)\n            && let [.., trait_segment, _method_segment] = path.segments\n            && expr.span.can_be_used_for_suggestions()\n        {\n            let span = path.span.shrink_to_lo().to(trait_segment.ident.span);\n            return ControlFlow::Break(errors::SuggestAnnotation::Path(span));\n        }\n\n        // Or else, try suggesting turbofishing the method args.\n        if let hir::ExprKind::MethodCall(segment, ..) = expr.kind\n            && let Some(def_id) =\n                self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().type_dependent_def_id(expr.hir_id)\n            && expr.span.can_be_used_for_suggestions()\n        {\n            self.suggest_for_segment(segment, def_id, expr.hir_id)?;\n        }\n\n        hir::intravisit::walk_expr(self, expr)\n    }\n\n    fn visit_local(&mut self, local: &'tcx hir::LetStmt<'tcx>) -> Self::Result {\n        // For a local, try suggest annotating the type if it's missing.\n        if let hir::LocalSource::Normal = local.source\n            && let None = local.ty\n            && let Some(ty) = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().node_type_opt(local.hir_id)\n            && let Some(vid) = self.fcx.root_vid(ty)\n            && self.reachable_vids.contains(&vid)\n            && local.span.can_be_used_for_suggestions()\n        {\n            return ControlFlow::Break(errors::SuggestAnnotation::Local(\n                local.pat.span.shrink_to_hi(),\n            ));\n        }\n        hir::intravisit::walk_local(self, local)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone)]\npub(crate) enum UnsafeUseReason {\n    Call,\n    Method,\n    Path,\n    UnionField,\n    Deref,\n}\n\n/// Finds all type variables which are passed to an `unsafe` operation.\n///\n/// For example, for this function `f`:\n/// ```ignore (demonstrative)\n/// fn f() {\n///     unsafe {\n///         let x /* ?X */ = core::mem::zeroed();\n///         //               ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ -- hir_id, span, reason\n///\n///         let y = core::mem::zeroed::<Option<_ /* ?Y */>>();\n///         //      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ -- hir_id, span, reason\n///     }\n/// }\n/// ```\n///\n/// `compute_unsafe_infer_vars` will return `{ id(?X) -> (hir_id, span, Call) }`\nfn compute_unsafe_infer_vars<'a, 'tcx>(\n    fcx: &'a FnCtxt<'a, 'tcx>,\n    body_id: LocalDefId,\n) -> UnordMap<ty::TyVid, (HirId, Span, UnsafeUseReason)> {\n    let body = fcx.tcx.hir_maybe_body_owned_by(body_id).expect(\"body id must have an owner\");\n    let mut res = UnordMap::default();\n\n    struct UnsafeInferVarsVisitor<'a, 'tcx> {\n        fcx: &'a FnCtxt<'a, 'tcx>,\n        res: &'a mut UnordMap<ty::TyVid, (HirId, Span, UnsafeUseReason)>,\n    }\n\n    impl Visitor<'_> for UnsafeInferVarsVisitor<'_, '_> {\n        fn visit_expr(&mut self, ex: &'_ hir::Expr<'_>) {\n            let typeck_results = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow();\n\n            match ex.kind {\n                hir::ExprKind::MethodCall(..) => {\n                    if let Some(def_id) = typeck_results.type_dependent_def_id(ex.hir_id)\n                        && let method_ty = self.fcx.tcx.type_of(def_id).instantiate_identity()\n                        && let sig = method_ty.fn_sig(self.fcx.tcx)\n                        && sig.safety().is_unsafe()\n                    {\n                        let mut collector = InferVarCollector {\n                            value: (ex.hir_id, ex.span, UnsafeUseReason::Method),\n                            res: self.res,\n                        };\n\n                        // Collect generic arguments (incl. `Self`) of the method\n                        typeck_results\n                            .node_args(ex.hir_id)\n                            .types()\n                            .for_each(|t| t.visit_with(&mut collector));\n                    }\n                }\n\n                hir::ExprKind::Call(func, ..) => {\n                    let func_ty = typeck_results.expr_ty(func);\n\n                    if func_ty.is_fn()\n                        && let sig = func_ty.fn_sig(self.fcx.tcx)\n                        && sig.safety().is_unsafe()\n                    {\n                        let mut collector = InferVarCollector {\n                            value: (ex.hir_id, ex.span, UnsafeUseReason::Call),\n                            res: self.res,\n                        };\n\n                        // Try collecting generic arguments of the function.\n                        // Note that we do this below for any paths (that don't have to be called),\n                        // but there we do it with a different span/reason.\n                        // This takes priority.\n                        typeck_results\n                            .node_args(func.hir_id)\n                            .types()\n                            .for_each(|t| t.visit_with(&mut collector));\n\n                        // Also check the return type, for cases like `returns_unsafe_fn_ptr()()`\n                        sig.output().visit_with(&mut collector);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Check paths which refer to functions.\n                // We do this, instead of only checking `Call` to make sure the lint can't be\n                // avoided by storing unsafe function in a variable.\n                hir::ExprKind::Path(_) => {\n                    let ty = typeck_results.expr_ty(ex);\n\n                    // If this path refers to an unsafe function, collect inference variables which may affect it.\n                    // `is_fn` excludes closures, but those can't be unsafe.\n                    if ty.is_fn()\n                        && let sig = ty.fn_sig(self.fcx.tcx)\n                        && sig.safety().is_unsafe()\n                    {\n                        let mut collector = InferVarCollector {\n                            value: (ex.hir_id, ex.span, UnsafeUseReason::Path),\n                            res: self.res,\n                        };\n\n                        // Collect generic arguments of the function\n                        typeck_results\n                            .node_args(ex.hir_id)\n                            .types()\n                            .for_each(|t| t.visit_with(&mut collector));\n                    }\n                }\n\n                hir::ExprKind::Unary(hir::UnOp::Deref, pointer) => {\n                    if let ty::RawPtr(pointee, _) = typeck_results.expr_ty(pointer).kind() {\n                        pointee.visit_with(&mut InferVarCollector {\n                            value: (ex.hir_id, ex.span, UnsafeUseReason::Deref),\n                            res: self.res,\n                        });\n                    }\n                }\n\n                hir::ExprKind::Field(base, _) => {\n                    let base_ty = typeck_results.expr_ty(base);\n\n                    if base_ty.is_union() {\n                        typeck_results.expr_ty(ex).visit_with(&mut InferVarCollector {\n                            value: (ex.hir_id, ex.span, UnsafeUseReason::UnionField),\n                            res: self.res,\n                        });\n                    }\n                }\n\n                _ => (),\n            };\n\n            hir::intravisit::walk_expr(self, ex);\n        }\n    }\n\n    struct ForDiagnostic,\n    /// Print the RTN type as an impl trait, i.e. `impl Sized`.\n    ForSignature,\n    /// Print the RTN type as a value path, i.e. `T::method(..): ...`.\n  )+\n    }\n}\n\ndefine_helper!(\n    /// Avoids running select queries during any prints that occur\n    /// during the closure. This may alter the appearance of some\n    /// types (e.g. forcing verbose printing for opaque types).\n    /// This method is used during some queries (e.g. `explicit_item_bounds`\n    /// for opaque types), to ensure that any debug printing that\n    /// occurs during the query computation does not end up recursively\n    /// calling the same query.\n    fn with_reduced_queries(ReducedQueriesGuard, REDUCED_QUERIES);\n    /// Force us to name impls with just the filename/line number. We\n    /// normally try to use types. But at some points, notably while printing\n    /// cycle errors, this can result in extra or suboptimal error output,\n    /// so this variable disables that check.\n    fn with_forced_impl_filename_line(ForcedImplGuard, FORCE_IMPL_FILENAME_LINE);\n    /// Adds the crate name prefix to paths where appropriate.\n    /// Unlike `with_crate_prefix`, this unconditionally uses `tcx.crate_name` instead of sometimes\n    /// using `crate::` for local items.\n    ///\n    /// Overrides `with_crate_prefix`.\n\n    // This function is not currently used in-tree, but it's used by a downstream rustc-driver in\n    // Ferrocene. Please check with them before removing it.\n    fn with_resolve_crate_name(CrateNamePrefixGuard, SHOULD_PREFIX_WITH_CRATE_NAME);\n    /// Adds the `crate::` prefix to paths where appropriate.\n    ///\n    /// Ignored if `with_resolve_crate_name` is active.\n    fn with_crate_prefix(CratePrefixGuard, SHOULD_PREFIX_WITH_CRATE);\n    /// Prevent path trimming if it is turned on. {\n    pub fn with(mode: RtnMode) -> RtnModeHelper {\n        RtnModeHelper(RTN_MODE.with(|c| c.replace(mode)))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Drop for RtnModeHelper {\n    fn drop(&mut self) {\n        RTN_MODE.with(|c| c.set(self.0))\n    }\n}\n\n/// Print types for the purposes of a suggestion.\n///\n/// Specifically, this will render RPITITs as `T::method(..)` which is suitable for\n/// things like where-clauses.\npub macro with_types_for_suggestion($e:expr) {{\n    let _guard = $crate::ty::print::pretty::RtnModeHelper::with(RtnMode::ForSuggestion);\n    $e\n}}\n\n/// Print types for the purposes of a signature suggestion.\n///\n/// Specifically, this will render RPITITs as `impl Trait` rather than `T::method(..)`.\npub macro with_types_for_signature($e:expr) {{\n    let _guard = $crate::ty::print::with_reduced_queries!($crate::ty::print::with_forced_impl_filename_line!(\n        $crate::ty::print::with_no_trimmed_paths!($crate::ty::print::with_no_visible_paths!(\n            $crate::ty::print::with_forced_impl_filename_line!($e)\n        ))\n    ))\n}}\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum WrapBinderMode {\n    ForAll,\n    Unsafe,\n}\nimpl WrapBinderMode {\n    pub fn start_str(self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            WrapBinderMode::ForAll => \"for<\",\n            WrapBinderMode::Unsafe => \"unsafe<\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// The \"region highlights\" are used to control region printing during\n/// specific error messages. When a \"region highlight\" is enabled, it\n/// gives an alternate way to print specific regions. For now, we\n/// always print those regions using a number, so something like \"`'0`\".\n///\n/// Regions not selected by the region highlight mode are presently\n/// unaffected.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Default)]\npub struct RegionHighlightMode<'tcx> {\n    /// If enabled, when we see the selected region, use \"`'N`\"\n    /// instead of the ordinary behavior.\n    highlight_regions: [Option<(ty::Region<'tcx>, usize)>; 3],\n\n    /// If enabled, when printing a \"free region\" that originated from\n    /// the given `ty::BoundRegionKind`, print it as \"`'1`\". Free regions that would ordinarily\n    /// have names print as normal.\n    ///\n    /// This is used when you have a signature like `fn foo(x: &u32,\n    /// y: &'a u32)` and we want to give a name to the region of the\n    /// reference `x`.\n    highlight_bound_region: Option<(ty::BoundRegionKind, usize)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> RegionHighlightMode<'tcx> {\n    /// If `region` and `number` are both `Some`, invokes\n    /// `highlighting_region`.\n    pub fn maybe_highlighting_region(\n        &mut self,\n        region: Option<ty::Region<'tcx>>,\n        number: Option<usize>,\n    ) {\n        if let Some(k) = region\n            && let Some(n) = number\n        {\n            self.highlighting_region(k, n);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Highlights the region inference variable `vid` as `'N`.\n    pub fn highlighting_region(&mut self, region: ty::Region<'tcx>, number: usize) {\n        let num_slots = self.highlight_regions.len();\n        let first_avail_slot =\n            self.highlight_regions.iter_mut().find(|s| s.is_none()).unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                bug!(\"can only highlight {} placeholders at a time\", num_slots,)\n            });\n        *first_avail_slot = Some((region, number));\n    }\n\n    /// Convenience wrapper for `highlighting_region`.\n    pub fn highlighting_region_vid(\n        &mut self,\n        tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n        vid: ty::RegionVid,\n        number: usize,\n    ) {\n        self.highlighting_region(ty::Region::new_var(tcx, vid), number)\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `Some(n)` with the number to use for the given region, if any.\n    fn region_highlighted(&self, region: ty::Region<'tcx>) -> Option<usize> {\n        self.highlight_regions.iter().find_map(|h| match h {\n            Some((r, n)) if *r == region => Some(*n),\n            _ => None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Highlight the given bound region.\n    /// We can only highlight one bound region at a time. See\n    /// the field `highlight_bound_region` for more detailed notes.\n    pub fn highlighting_bound_region(&mut self, br: ty::BoundRegionKind, number: usize) {\n        assert!(self.highlight_bound_region.is_none());\n        self.highlight_bound_region = Some((br, number));\n    }\n}\n\n/// Trait for printers that pretty-print using `fmt::Write` to the printer.\npub trait PrettyPrinter<'tcx>: Printer<'tcx> + fmt::Write {\n    /// Like `print_def_path` but for value paths.\n    fn pretty_print_value_path(\n        &mut self,\n        def_id: DefId,\n        args: &'tcx [GenericArg<'tcx>],\n    ) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n        self.print_def_path(def_id, args)\n    }\n\n    fn pretty_print_in_binder<T>(&mut self, value: &ty::Binder<'tcx, T>) -> Result<(), PrintError>\n    where\n        T: Print<'tcx, Self> + TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        value.as_ref().skip_binder().print(self)\n    }\n\n    fn wrap_binder<T, F: FnOnce(&T, &mut Self) -> Result<(), fmt::Error>>(\n        &mut self,\n        value: &ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n        _mode: WrapBinderMode,\n        f: F,\n    ) -> Result<(), PrintError>\n    where\n        T: TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        f(value.as_ref().skip_binder(), self)\n    }\n\n    /// Prints comma-separated elements.\n    fn comma_sep<T>(&mut self, mut elems: impl Iterator<Item = T>) -> Result<(), PrintError>\n    where\n        T: Print<'tcx, Self>,\n    {\n        if let Some(first) = elems.next() {\n            first.print(self)?;\n            for elem in elems {\n                self.write_str(\", \")?;\n                elem.print(self)?;\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Prints `{f: t}` or `{f as t}` depending on the `cast` argument\n    fn typed_value(\n        &mut self,\n        f: impl FnOnce(&mut Self) -> Result<(), PrintError>,\n        t: impl FnOnce(&mut Self) -> Result<(), PrintError>,\n        conversion: &str,\n    ) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n        self.write_str(\"{\")?;\n        f(self)?;\n        self.write_str(conversion)?;\n        t(self)?;\n        self.write_str(\"}\")?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Prints `(...)` around what `f` prints.\n    fn parenthesized(\n        &mut self,\n        f: impl FnOnce(&mut Self) -> Result<(), PrintError>,\n    ) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n        self.write_str(\"(\")?;\n        f(self)?;\n        self.write_str(\")\")?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Prints `(...)` around what `f` prints if `parenthesized` is true, otherwise just prints `f`.\n    fn maybe_parenthesized(\n        &mut self,\n        f: impl FnOnce(&mut Self) -> Result<(), PrintError>,\n        parenthesized: bool,\n    ) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n        if parenthesized {\n            self.parenthesized(f)?;\n        } else {\n            f(self)?;\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Prints `<...>` around what `f` prints.\n    fn generic_delimiters(\n        &mut self,\n        f: impl FnOnce(&mut Self) -> Result<(), PrintError>,\n    ) -> Result<(), PrintError>;\n\n    fn should_truncate(&mut self) -> bool {\n        false\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if the region should be printed in optional positions,\n    /// e.g., `&'a T` or `dyn Tr + 'b`. (Regions like the one in `Cow<'static, T>`\n    /// will always be printed.)\n    fn should_print_optional_region(&self, region: ty::Region<'tcx>) -> bool;\n\n    fn reset_type_limit(&mut self) {}\n\n    // Defaults (should not be overridden):\n\n    /// If possible, this returns a global path resolving to `def_id` that is visible\n    /// from at least one local module, and returns `true`. If the crate defining `def_id` is\n    /// declared with an `extern crate`, the path is guaranteed to use the `extern crate`.\n    fn try_print_visible_def_path(&mut self, def_id: DefId) -> Result<bool, PrintError> {\n        if with_no_visible_paths() {\n            return Ok(false);\n        }\n\n        let mut callers = Vec::new();\n        self.try_print_visible_def_path_recur(def_id, &mut callers)\n    }\n\n    // Given a `DefId`, produce a short name. For types and traits, it prints *only* its name,\n    // For associated items on traits it prints out the trait's name and the associated item's name.\n    // For enum variants, if they have an unique name, then we only print the name, otherwise we\n    // print the enum name and the variant name. Otherwise, we do not print anything and let the\n    // caller use the `print_def_path` fallback.\n    fn force_print_trimmed_def_path(&mut self, def_id: DefId) -> Result<bool, PrintError> {\n        let key = self.tcx().def_key(def_id);\n        let visible_parent_map = self.tcx().visible_parent_map(());\n        let kind = self.tcx().def_kind(def_id);\n\n        let get_local_name = |this: &Self, name, def_id, key: DefKey| {\n            if let Some(visible_parent) = visible_parent_map.get(&def_id)\n                && let actual_parent = this.tcx().opt_parent(def_id)\n                && let DefPathData::TypeNs(_) = key.disambiguated_data.data\n                && Some(*visible_parent) != actual_parent\n            {\n                this.tcx()\n                    // FIXME(typed_def_id): Further propagate ModDefId\n                    .module_children(ModDefId::new_unchecked(*visible_parent))\n                    .iter()\n                    .filter(|child| child.res.opt_def_id() == Some(def_id))\n                    .find(|child| child.vis.is_public() && child.ident.name != kw::Underscore)\n                    .map(|child| child.ident.name)\n                    .unwrap_or(name)\n            } else {\n                name\n            }\n        };\n        if let DefKind::Variant = kind\n            && let Some(symbol) = self.tcx().trimmed_def_paths(()).get(&def_id)\n        {\n            // If `Assoc` is unique, we don't want to talk about `Trait::Assoc`.\n            self.write_str(get_local_name(self, *symbol, def_id, key).as_str())?;\n            return Ok(true);\n        }\n        if let Some(symbol) = key.get_opt_name() {\n            if let DefKind::AssocConst | DefKind::AssocFn | DefKind::AssocTy = kind\n                && let Some(parent) = self.tcx().opt_parent(def_id)\n                && let parent_key = self.tcx().def_key(parent)\n                && let Some(symbol) = parent_key.get_opt_name()\n            {\n                // Trait\n                self.write_str(get_local_name(self, symbol, parent, parent_key).as_str())?;\n                self.write_str(\"::\")?;\n            } else if let DefKind::Variant = kind\n                && let Some(parent) = self.tcx().opt_parent(def_id)\n                && let parent_key = self.tcx().def_key(parent)\n                && let Some(symbol) = parent_key.get_opt_name()\n            {\n                // Enum\n\n                // For associated items and variants, we want the \"full\" path, namely, include\n                // the parent type in the path. For example, `Iterator::Item`.\n                self.write_str(get_local_name(self, symbol, parent, parent_key).as_str())?;\n                self.write_str(\"::\")?;\n            } else if let DefKind::Struct\n            | DefKind::Union\n            | DefKind::Enum\n            | DefKind::Trait\n            | DefKind::TyAlias\n            | DefKind::Fn\n            | DefKind::Const\n            | DefKind::Static { .. } = kind\n            {\n            } else {\n                // If not covered above, like for example items out of `impl` blocks, fallback.\n                return Ok(false);\n            }\n            self.write_str(get_local_name(self, symbol, def_id, key).as_str())?;\n            return Ok(true);\n        }\n        Ok(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Try to see if this path can be trimmed to a unique symbol name.\n    fn try_print_trimmed_def_path(&mut self, def_id: DefId) -> Result<bool, PrintError> {\n        if with_forced_trimmed_paths() && self.force_print_trimmed_def_path(def_id)? {\n            return Ok(true);\n        }\n        if self.tcx().sess.opts.unstable_opts.trim_diagnostic_paths\n            && self.tcx().sess.opts.trimmed_def_paths\n            && !with_no_trimmed_paths()\n            && !with_crate_prefix()\n            && let Some(symbol) = self.tcx().trimmed_def_paths(()).get(&def_id)\n        {\n            write!(self, \"{}\", Ident::with_dummy_span(*symbol))?;\n            Ok(true)\n        } else {\n            Ok(false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Does the work of `try_print_visible_def_path`, building the\n    /// full definition path recursively before attempting to\n    /// post-process it into the valid and visible version that\n    /// accounts for re-exports.\n    ///\n    /// This method should only be called by itself or\n    /// `try_print_visible_def_path`.\n    ///\n    /// `callers` is a chain of visible_parent's leading to `def_id`,\n    /// to support cycle detection during recursion.\n    ///\n    /// This method returns false if we can't print the visible path, so\n    /// `print_def_path` can fall back on the item's real definition path.\n    fn try_print_visible_def_path_recur(\n        &mut self,\n        def_id: DefId,\n        callers: &mut Vec<DefId>,\n    ) -> Result<bool, PrintError> {\n        debug!(\"try_print_visible_def_path: def_id={:?}\", def_id);\n\n        // If `def_id` is a direct or injected extern crate, return the\n        // path to the crate followed by the path to the item within the crate.\n        if let Some(cnum) = def_id.as_crate_root() {\n            if cnum == LOCAL_CRATE {\n                self.print_crate_name(cnum)?;\n                return Ok(true);\n            }\n\n            // In local mode, when we encounter a crate other than\n            // LOCAL_CRATE, execution proceeds in one of two ways:\n            //\n            // 1. For a direct dependency, where user added an\n            //    `extern crate` manually, we put the `extern\n            //    crate` as the parent. So you wind up with\n            //    something relative to the current crate.\n            // 2. For an extern inferred from a path or an indirect crate,\n            //    where there is no explicit `extern crate`, we just prepend\n            //    the crate name.\n            match self.tcx().extern_crate(cnum) {\n                Some(&ExternCrate { src, dependency_of, span, .. }) => match (src, dependency_of) {\n                    (ExternCrateSource::Extern(def_id), LOCAL_CRATE) => {\n                        // NOTE(eddyb) the only reason `span` might be dummy,\n                        // that we're aware of, is that it's the `std`/`core`\n                        // `extern crate` injected by default.\n                        // FIXME(eddyb) find something better to key this on,\n                        // or avoid ending up with `ExternCrateSource::Extern`,\n                        // for the injected `std`/`core`.\n                        if span.is_dummy() {\n                            self.print_crate_name(cnum)?;\n                            return Ok(true);\n                        }\n\n                        // Disable `try_print_trimmed_def_path` behavior within\n                        // the `print_def_path` call, to avoid infinite recursion\n                        // in cases where the `extern crate foo` has non-trivial\n                        // parents, e.g. it's nested in `impl foo::Trait for Bar`\n                        // (see also issues #55779 and #87932).\n                        with_no_visible_paths!(self.print_def_path(def_id, &[])?);\n\n                        return Ok(true);\n                    }\n                    (ExternCrateSource::Path, LOCAL_CRATE) => {\n                        self.print_crate_name(cnum)?;\n                        return Ok(true);\n                    }\n                    _ => {}\n                },\n                None => {\n                    self.print_crate_name(cnum)?;\n                    return Ok(true);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        if def_id.is_local() {\n            return Ok(false);\n        }\n\n        let visible_parent_map = self.tcx().visible_parent_map(());\n\n        let mut cur_def_key = self.tcx().def_key(def_id);\n        debug!(\"try_print_visible_def_path: cur_def_key={:?}\", cur_def_key);\n\n        // For a constructor, we want the name of its parent rather than <unnamed>.\n        if let DefPathData::Ctor = cur_def_key.disambiguated_data.data {\n            let parent = DefId {\n                krate: def_id.krate,\n                index: cur_def_key\n                    .parent\n                    .expect(\"`DefPathData::Ctor` / `VariantData` missing a parent\"),\n            };\n\n            cur_def_key = self.tcx().def_key(parent);\n        }\n\n        let Some(visible_parent) = visible_parent_map.get(&def_id).cloned() else {\n            return Ok(false);\n        };\n\n        if self.tcx().is_doc_hidden(visible_parent) && with_no_visible_paths_if_doc_hidden() {\n            return Ok(false);\n        }\n\n        let actual_parent = self.tcx().opt_parent(def_id);\n        debug!(\n            \"try_print_visible_def_path: visible_parent={:?} actual_parent={:?}\",\n            visible_parent, actual_parent,\n        );\n\n        let mut data = cur_def_key.disambiguated_data.data;\n        debug!(\n            \"try_print_visible_def_path: data={:?} visible_parent={:?} actual_parent={:?}\",\n            data, visible_parent, actual_parent,\n        );\n\n        match data {\n            // In order to output a path that could actually be imported (valid and visible),\n            // we need to handle re-exports correctly.\n            //\n            // For example, take `std::os::unix::process::CommandExt`, this trait is actually\n            // defined at `std::sys::unix::ext::process::CommandExt` (at time of writing).\n            //\n            // `std::os::unix` reexports the contents of `std::sys::unix::ext`. `std::sys` is\n            // private so the \"true\" path to `CommandExt` isn't accessible.\n            //\n            // In this case, the `visible_parent_map` will look something like this:\n            //\n            // (child) -> (parent)\n            // `std::sys::unix::ext::process::CommandExt` -> `std::sys::unix::ext::process`\n            // `std::sys::unix::ext::process` -> `std::sys::unix::ext`\n            // `std::sys::unix::ext` -> `std::os`\n            //\n            // This is correct, as the visible parent of `std::sys::unix::ext` is in fact\n            // `std::os`.\n            //\n            // When printing the path to `CommandExt` and looking at the `cur_def_key` that\n            // corresponds to `std::sys::unix::ext`, we would normally print `ext` and then go\n            // to the parent - resulting in a mangled path like\n            // `std::os::ext::process::CommandExt`.\n            //\n            // Instead, we must detect that there was a re-export and instead print `unix`\n            // (which is the name `std::sys::unix::ext` was re-exported as in `std::os`). To\n            // do this, we compare the parent of `std::sys::unix::ext` (`std::sys::unix`) with\n            // the visible parent (`std::os`). If these do not match, then we iterate over\n            // the children of the visible parent (as was done when computing\n            // `visible_parent_map`), looking for the specific child we currently have and then\n            // have access to the re-exported name.\n            DefPathData::TypeNs(ref mut name) if Some(visible_parent) != actual_parent => {\n                // Item might be re-exported several times, but filter for the one\n                // that's public and whose identifier isn't `_`.\n                let reexport = self\n                    .tcx()\n                    // FIXME(typed_def_id): Further propagate ModDefId\n                    .module_children(ModDefId::new_unchecked(visible_parent))\n                    .iter()\n                    .filter(|child| child.res.opt_def_id() == Some(def_id))\n                    .find(|child| child.vis.is_public() && child.ident.name != kw::Underscore)\n                    .map(|child| child.ident.name);\n\n                if let Some(new_name) = reexport {\n                    *name = new_name;\n                } else {\n                    // There is no name that is public and isn't `_`, so bail.\n                    return Ok(false);\n                }\n            }\n            // Re-exported `extern crate` (#43189).\n            DefPathData::CrateRoot => {\n                data = DefPathData::TypeNs(self.tcx().crate_name(def_id.krate));\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n        debug!(\"try_print_visible_def_path: data={:?}\", data);\n\n        if callers.contains(&visible_parent) {\n            return Ok(false);\n        }\n        callers.push(visible_parent);\n        // HACK(eddyb) this bypasses `print_path_with_simple`'s prefix printing to avoid\n        // knowing ahead of time whether the entire path will succeed or not.\n        // To support printers that do not implement `PrettyPrinter`, a `Vec` or\n        // linked list on the stack would need to be built, before any printing.\n        match self.try_print_visible_def_path_recur(visible_parent, callers)? {\n            false => return Ok(false),\n            true => {}\n        }\n        callers.pop();\n        self.print_path_with_simple(\n            |_| Ok(()),\n            &DisambiguatedDefPathData { data, disambiguator: 0 },\n        )?;\n        Ok(true)\n    }\n\n    fn pretty_print_path_with_qualified(\n        &mut self,\n        self_ty: Ty<'tcx>,\n        trait_ref: Option<ty::TraitRef<'tcx>>,\n    ) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n        if trait_ref.is_none() {\n            // Inherent impls. Try to print `Foo::bar` for an inherent\n            // impl on `Foo`, but fallback to `<Foo>::bar` if self-type is\n            // anything other than a simple path.\n            match self_ty.kind() {\n                ty::Adt(..)\n                | ty::Foreign(_)\n                | ty::Bool\n                | ty::Char\n                | ty::Str\n                | ty::Int(_)\n                | ty::Uint(_)\n                | ty::Float(_) => {\n                    return self_ty.print(self);\n                }\n\n                _ => {}\n            }\n        }\n\n        self.generic_delimiters(|p| {\n            self_ty.print(p)?;\n            if let Some(trait_ref) = trait_ref {\n                write!(p, \" as \")?;\n                trait_ref.print_only_trait_path().print(p)?;\n            }\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn pretty_print_path_with_impl(\n        &mut self,\n        print_prefix: impl FnOnce(&mut Self) -> Result<(), PrintError>,\n        self_ty: Ty<'tcx>,\n        trait_ref: Option<ty::TraitRef<'tcx>>,\n    ) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n        print_prefix(self)?;\n\n        self.generic_delimiters(|p| {\n            write!(p, \"impl \")?;\n            if let Some(trait_ref) = trait_ref {\n                trait_ref.print_only_trait_path().print(p)?;\n                write!(p, \" for \")?;\n            }\n            self_ty.print(p)?;\n\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn pretty_print_type(&mut self, ty: Ty<'tcx>) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n        match *ty.kind() {\n            ty::Bool => write!(self, \"bool\")?,\n            ty::Char => write!(self, \"char\")?,\n            ty::Int(t) => write!(self, \"{}\", t.name_str())?,\n            ty::Uint(t) => write!(self, \"{}\", t.name_str())?,\n            ty::Float(t) => write!(self, \"{}\", t.name_str())?,\n            ty::Pat(ty, pat) => {\n                write!(self, \"(\")?;\n                ty.print(self)?;\n                write!(self, \") is {pat:?}\")?;\n            }\n            ty::RawPtr(ty, mutbl) => {\n                write!(self, \"*{} \", mutbl.ptr_str())?;\n                ty.print(self)?;\n            }\n            ty::Ref(r, ty, mutbl) => {\n                write!(self, \"&\")?;\n                if self.should_print_optional_region(r) {\n                    r.print(self)?;\n                    write!(self, \" \")?;\n                }\n                ty::TypeAndMut { ty, mutbl }.print(self)?;\n            }\n            ty::Never => write!(self, \"!\")?,\n            ty::Tuple(tys) => {\n                write!(self, \"(\")?;\n                self.comma_sep(tys.iter())?;\n                if tys.len() == 1 {\n                    write!(self, \",\")?;\n                }\n                write!(self, \")\")?;\n            }\n            ty::FnDef(def_id, args) => {\n                if with_reduced_queries() {\n                    self.print_def_path(def_id, args)?;\n                } else {\n                    let mut sig = self.tcx().fn_sig(def_id).instantiate(self.tcx(), args);\n                    if self.tcx().codegen_fn_attrs(def_id).safe_target_features {\n                        write!(self, \"#[target_features] \")?;\n                        sig = sig.map_bound(|mut sig| {\n                            sig.safety = hir::Safety::Safe;\n                            sig\n                        });\n                    }\n                    sig.print(self)?;\n                    write!(self, \" {{\")?;\n                    self.pretty_print_value_path(def_id, args)?;\n                    write!(self, \"}}\")?;\n                }\n            }\n            ty::FnPtr(ref sig_tys, hdr) => sig_tys.with(hdr).print(self)?,\n            ty::UnsafeBinder(ref bound_ty) => {\n                self.wrap_binder(bound_ty, WrapBinderMode::Unsafe, |ty, p| {\n                    p.pretty_print_type(*ty)\n                })?;\n            }\n            ty::Infer(infer_ty) => {\n                if self.should_print_verbose() {\n                    write!(self, \"{:?}\", ty.kind())?;\n                    return Ok(());\n                }\n\n                if let ty::TyVar(ty_vid) = infer_ty {\n                    if let Some(name) = self.ty_infer_name(ty_vid) {\n                        write!(self, \"{name}\")?;\n                    } else {\n                        write!(self, \"{infer_ty}\")?;\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    write!(self, \"{infer_ty}\")?;\n                }\n            }\n            ty::Error(_) => write!(self, \"{{type error}}\")?,\n            ty::Param(ref param_ty) => param_ty.print(self)?,\n            ty::Bound(debruijn, bound_ty) => match bound_ty.kind {\n                ty::BoundTyKind::Anon => {\n                    rustc_type_ir::debug_bound_var(self, debruijn, bound_ty.var)?\n                }\n                ty::BoundTyKind::Param(def_id) => match self.should_print_verbose() {\n                    true => write!(self, \"{:?}\", ty.kind())?,\n                    false => write!(self, \"{}\", self.tcx().item_name(def_id))?,\n                },\n            },\n            ty::Adt(def, args) => self.print_def_path(def.did(), args)?,\n            ty::Dynamic(data, r) => {\n                let print_r = self.should_print_optional_region(r);\n                if print_r {\n                    write!(self, \"(\")?;\n                }\n                write!(self, \"dyn \")?;\n                data.print(self)?;\n                if print_r {\n                    write!(self, \" + \")?;\n                    r.print(self)?;\n                    write!(self, \")\")?;\n                }\n            }\n            ty::Foreign(def_id) => self.print_def_path(def_id, &[])?,\n            ty::Alias(ty::Projection | ty::Inherent | ty::Free, ref data) => data.print(self)?,\n            ty::Placeholder(placeholder) => placeholder.print(self)?,\n            ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id, args, .. }) => {\n                // We use verbose printing in 'NO_QUERIES' mode, to\n                // avoid needing to call `predicates_of`. This should\n                // only affect certain debug messages (e.g. messages printed\n                // from `rustc_middle::ty` during the computation of `tcx.predicates_of`),\n                // and should have no effect on any compiler output.\n                // [Unless `-Zverbose-internals` is used, e.g. in the output of\n                // `tests/ui/nll/ty-outlives/impl-trait-captures.rs`, for\n                // example.]\n                if self.should_print_verbose() {\n                    // FIXME(eddyb) print this with `print_def_path`.\n                    write!(self, \"Opaque({:?}, {})\", def_id, args.print_as_list())?;\n                    return Ok(());\n                }\n\n                let parent = self.tcx().parent(def_id);\n                match self.tcx().def_kind(parent) {\n                    DefKind::TyAlias | DefKind::AssocTy => {\n                        // NOTE: I know we should check for NO_QUERIES here, but it's alright.\n                        // `type_of` on a type alias or assoc type should never cause a cycle.\n                        if let ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: d, .. }) =\n                            *self.tcx().type_of(parent).instantiate_identity().kind()\n                        {\n                            if d == def_id {\n                                // If the type alias directly starts with the `impl` of the\n                                // opaque type we're printing, then skip the `::{opaque#1}`.\n                                self.print_def_path(parent, args)?;\n                                return Ok(());\n                            }\n                        }\n                        // Complex opaque type, e.g. `type Foo = (i32, impl Debug);`\n                        self.print_def_path(def_id, args)?;\n                        return Ok(());\n                    }\n                    _ => {\n                        if with_reduced_queries() {\n                            self.print_def_path(def_id, &[])?;\n                            return Ok(());\n                        } else {\n                            return self.pretty_print_opaque_impl_type(def_id, args);\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            ty::Str => write!(self, \"str\")?,\n            ty::Coroutine(did, args) => {\n                write!(self, \"{{\")?;\n                let coroutine_kind = self.tcx().coroutine_kind(did).unwrap();\n                let should_print_movability = self.should_print_verbose()\n                    || matches!(coroutine_kind, hir::CoroutineKind::Coroutine(_));\n\n                if should_print_movability {\n                    match coroutine_kind.movability() {\n                        hir::Movability::Movable => {}\n                        hir::Movability::Static => write!(self, \"static \")?,\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if !self.should_print_verbose() {\n                    write!(self, \"{coroutine_kind}\")?;\n                    if coroutine_kind.is_fn_like() {\n                        // If we are printing an `async fn` coroutine type, then give the path\n                        // of the fn, instead of its span, because that will in most cases be\n                        // more helpful for the reader than just a source location.\n                        //\n                        // This will look like:\n                        //    {async fn body of some_fn()}\n                        let did_of_the_fn_item = self.tcx().parent(did);\n                        write!(self, \" of \")?;\n                        self.print_def_path(did_of_the_fn_item, args)?;\n                        write!(self, \"()\")?;\n                    } else if let Some(local_did) = did.as_local() {\n                        let span = self.tcx().def_span(local_did);\n                        write!(\n                            self,\n                            \"@{}\",\n                            // This may end up in stderr diagnostics but it may also be emitted\n                            // into MIR. Hence we use the remapped path if available\n                            self.tcx().sess.source_map().span_to_embeddable_string(span)\n                        )?;\n                    } else {\n                        write!(self, \"@\")?;\n                        self.print_def_path(did, args)?;\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    self.print_def_path(did, args)?;\n                    write!(self, \" upvar_tys=\")?;\n                    args.as_coroutine().tupled_upvars_ty().print(self)?;\n                    write!(self, \" resume_ty=\")?;\n                    args.as_coroutine().resume_ty().print(self)?;\n                    write!(self, \" yield_ty=\")?;\n                    args.as_coroutine().yield_ty().print(self)?;\n                    write!(self, \" return_ty=\")?;\n                    args.as_coroutine().return_ty().print(self)?;\n                }\n\n                write!(self, \"}}\")?\n            }\n            ty::CoroutineWitness(did, args) => {\n                write!(self, \"{{\")?;\n                if !self.tcx().sess.verbose_internals() {\n                    write!(self, \"coroutine witness\")?;\n                    if let Some(did) = did.as_local() {\n                        let span = self.tcx().def_span(did);\n                        write!(\n                            self,\n                            \"@{}\",\n                            // This may end up in stderr diagnostics but it may also be emitted\n                            // into MIR. Hence we use the remapped path if available\n                            self.tcx().sess.source_map().span_to_embeddable_string(span)\n                        )?;\n                    } else {\n                        write!(self, \"@\")?;\n                        self.print_def_path(did, args)?;\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    self.print_def_path(did, args)?;\n                }\n\n                write!(self, \"}}\")?\n            }\n            ty::Closure(did, args) => {\n                write!(self, \"{{\")?;\n                if !self.should_print_verbose() {\n                    write!(self, \"closure\")?;\n                    if self.should_truncate() {\n                        write!(self, \"@...}}\")?;\n                        return Ok(());\n                    } else {\n                        if let Some(did) = did.as_local() {\n                            if self.tcx().sess.opts.unstable_opts.span_free_formats {\n                                write!(self, \"@\")?;\n                                self.print_def_path(did.to_def_id(), args)?;\n                            } else {\n                                let span = self.tcx().def_span(did);\n                                let preference = if with_forced_trimmed_paths() {\n                                    FileNameDisplayPreference::Short\n                                } else {\n                                    FileNameDisplayPreference::Remapped\n                                };\n                                write!(\n                                    self,\n                                    \"@{}\",\n                                    // This may end up in stderr diagnostics but it may also be\n                                    // emitted into MIR. Hence we use the remapped path if\n                                    // available\n                                    self.tcx().sess.source_map().span_to_string(span, preference)\n                                )?;\n                            }\n                        } else {\n                            write!(self, \"@\")?;\n                            self.print_def_path(did, args)?;\n
